    Dietary Guidelines

    A postpartum woman should be careful with hot medicines and hot foods. Generally, food should be consumed warmer, similar to the body's temperature.

    Stroke

    For postpartum conditions like the involuntary reflex of the limbs and other wind diseases, harmful medicines should not be used; only one or two simple herbs are appropriate. Profuse sweating should also be avoided. Particularly, it is essential to refrain from excessive diarrhea or vomiting, which can lead to certain death. A soup made from purple soybeans is highly beneficial postpartum.

    Methods of Medication Postpartum

    After childbirth, women may experience alternating chills and fever, heart and chest fullness, joint pain, and severe headaches, which may worsen in the afternoon, appearing similar to malaria; in such cases, the Shuqi soup is advisable. For women who catch a cold during confinement with irritability in the limbs and headaches, Xiao Chai Hu decoction is recommended. If headaches are absent but there is restlessness, the San Wu Huangqin soup is appropriate. For women with headaches, body heat, and abdominal cramping pain after childbirth, Gui Xin and Muli decoction is suitable. For women feeling weak and experiencing chills and fever, reduced appetite, and bloating, the Zengsun Chai Hu decoction should be used.[7p]

    妇人产毕，饮热童便一盏，闭目少坐，上床倚高，立膝仰卧，不时唤醒，及以酢涂鼻，或用酢炭及烧漆器，更以手从心斡至脐下，使恶露不滞，如此三日，以防血运血逆。酒虽行血，亦不可多，恐引血入四肢，且能昏运，宜频食白粥少许；一月之后，宜食羊肉猪蹄少许；仍慎言语七情寒暑，梳头洗足，以百日为度。若气血素弱者，不计日月，否则患手足腰腿酸痛等证，名曰蓐劳，最难治疗。初产时，不可问是男是女，恐因言语而泄气，或以爱憎而动气，皆能致病；不可独宿，恐致虚惊；不可刮舌，恐伤心气；不可刷齿，恐致血逆。须血气平复，方可治事。犯恃微若秋毫，成病重如山岳，可不戒哉！　　【注　按《大全》：凡生产毕不得便卧，且宜闭目而坐，须臾上床，宜仰卧不宜侧卧，宜坚膝未可伸足，高倚床头，厚铺裀褥，遮围四壁，使无孔隙，免致贼风。不问腹痛不痛，有病无病，以童便和酒半盏温服五七服妙。但酒虽行血，亦不可多。】[8p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[8p]

    After a woman gives birth, she should drink a cup of warm urine from a child, close her eyes and sit quietly for a while. She should then lie down on the bed, leaning back, and remain on her knees while lying supine. If she wakes up occasionally, she should apply vinegar to her nose, or use a mixture of vinegar and charcoal, as well as burn lacquered utensils. Additionally, she should massage from her heart to below the navel to prevent postpartum discharge from stagnating. This should be done for three days to prevent blood circulation issues.

    Although alcohol promotes blood flow, it should not be consumed in excess, as it may cause blood to pool in the extremities and can lead to dizziness. It is advisable to frequently consume small amounts of plain porridge; after one month, she may start eating a little mutton and pig trotters. She should also be cautious about her words and emotions regarding temperature changes, comb her hair, and wash her feet, using a hundred days as a guideline.

    If her vital energy and blood are naturally weak, she should not be overly concerned about the days and months, as this could lead to symptoms of soreness in the hands, feet, waist, and legs, collectively referred to as “puerperal syndrome,” which is difficult to treat.

    During the early postpartum period, she should not inquire whether the child is a boy or a girl, lest her words cause unforeseen consequences, or allow emotions of love and hatred to disturb her, which can lead to illness. She should not sleep alone out of fear of fright; she should not scrape her tongue as it may harm her vital energy; nor should she brush her teeth, as it might cause blood to flow in reverse.

    She must restore her blood flow and vital energy before attending to other matters. If she makes even the slightest mistakes, the consequence could be serious, akin to falling into a deep pit.

    (Note: According to the “Great Compendium”: After childbirth, one must not lie down too easily; it is best to sit quietly with closed eyes, then lie down on the back rather than on the side, keeping the knees bent instead of extending the legs, leaning against the head of the bed with thick bedding to shield from drafts. Whether feeling abdominal pain or not, if sick or well, drink half a cup of warm urine and wine at five or seven intervals. However, alcohol, though it promotes blood flow, should still be consumed in moderation.)[8p]

    调理法

    　　产后将息如法，脏腑调和，庶无诸疾苦，须先服黑神散、四物汤、四顺理中丸、七宝散。若壮热头痛，此乳脉将行，用玉露散。头目不清是血运，用清魂散。粥食不美是胃气虚，用四顺理中丸。　　 【注　按丹溪先生云：四顺理中丸虽滋补，但干姜僭热，脏腑无寒，何处消受？其黑神散，产妇无病者，余亦不用。】[9p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[9p]

    Regulation Method

    After childbirth, one should rest according to the proper methods, harmonizing the organs to avoid various ailments. First, one must take Heisheng Powder, Siwu Decoction, Sishun Lichung Pill, and Qibao Powder. If there is strong heat and headache, this indicates the milk is about to flow; use Yulu Powder. If there is cloudiness in the head and eyes, it suggests blood circulation issues; use Qinghun Powder. If the porridge is not appealing, it indicates qi deficiency in the stomach; use Sishun Lichung Pill.

    Note: According to Master Danxi, while Sishun Lichung Pill nourishes, dry ginger may induce heat, and if the organs are not cold, where would it be absorbed? For Heisheng Powder, postpartum women without ailments should also refrain from its use.[9p]

    血运

    　　产后血运，乃血入肝经，甚至眼花昏闷，用黑神散主之。下血过多，用清魂散补之。或以酢汤细饮；或预烧秤锤以酢沃之，或酽酢涂口鼻或烧漆器熏之，使产母鼻吸其气，庶无此患。　　【注　产后元气亏损，恶露乘虚上攻，眼花头运，或心下满闷，神昏口噤，或痰壅盛者，急用失笑散主之。若血下多而运，或神昏烦乱者，大剂芎归汤补之；或芸薹子散，或童子小便；有痰加二陈汤。若因劳心力而致者，宜补中益气加香附。若因气血虚极，不省人事，用清魂散，继以芎归汤及大补气血之剂。凡产可用半夏末，冷水和丸入鼻孔中，并无前患。丹溪先生云：因气血俱虚，痰火泛上，宜以二陈导痰，或加减朱砂安神丸，以麦门冬汤下亦可。若脾胃虚而不能固者，用六君子汤。总之妊娠至五七个月，宜服安胎饮；至八九个月，再加大腹皮、黄杨脑。元气虚弱八珍汤。临产时更宜服保生无忧散，则无前患。[10p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[10p]

    Blood Circulation

    After childbirth, blood circulation involves blood entering the liver channel, which can lead to symptoms such as dizziness and mental fog. In such cases, the Black Spirit Powder should be administered. If there is excessive bleeding, it should be supplemented with the Clear Soul Powder. Remedies include drinking vinegar broth in small sips; pre-heating scales and rinsing them with vinegar; applying concentrated vinegar to the mouth and nose; or using lacquer utensils to diffuse the scent so that the mother can inhale it, which should prevent these issues.

    Note: After delivery, when the original energy is depleted, lochia may rise to cause dizziness, fullness and oppression in the chest, mental fog, or silence of speech, or if phlegm is abundant, the Lost Laughter Powder should be urgently used. If there is excessive bleeding and circulation, or mental confusion, a large dose of Chuanxiong Gui Decoction should be used; alternatively, the Cauliflower Seed Powder or a boy's urine can be employed. If there is phlegm, add the Er Chen Decoction. If it results from mental or physical exertion, one should take the Middle-Boosting Energy with added Xiangfu. If due to severe deficiency of qi and blood, and loss of consciousness, use the Clear Soul Powder, followed by Chuanxiong Gui Decoction and large doses to replenish qi and blood.

    In all cases post-delivery, powdered Banxia may be used, mixed with cold water into balls and inserted into the nostrils, with no prior issues. As Danxi says: if both qi and blood are deficient, and phlegm fire rises, it is advisable to use Er Chen to guide phlegm, or add or reduce Zhu Sha An Shen Pills, and Macleya Decoction is also acceptable. If the spleen and stomach are weak and cannot fortify, then use Liu Junzi Decoction.

    Overall, during pregnancy, especially at five to seven months, it is advisable to consume the An Tai Decoction; at eight or nine months, add Da Fu Pi and Huang Yang Nao. For weakened original energy, use Ba Zhen Decoction. At the time of delivery, it is particularly important to take the Bao Sheng Wu You Powder to prevent prior issues.[10p]

    】　　【按《大全》：产后血运者，由败血流入肝经，眼中黑花，头目旋运，不能起坐，甚致昏闷不省人事；谓之血运，细酒调黑神散最佳。庸医或作暗风中景治之。凡血运热乘虚，逆上凑心，故昏迷不省，气闭欲绝，然其由有三：有用心使力过多而运，有下血多而运，有下血少而运。其运虽同，治之则异，当详审之。下血多而运者，但昏闷烦乱而已，当以补血清心药。下血少而运者，乃恶露不下，上抢于心，心下满急，神昏口噤，绝不知人，当以破血行血药。[11p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[11p]

    According to the "Comprehensive Treatise": The blood circulation disorder after childbirth occurs when stagnant blood flows into the liver meridian, causing black spots in the eyes, dizziness, inability to sit up, and even fainting and loss of consciousness; this is called blood circulation disorder. The best remedy is a fine wine mixed with Bluespray Powder. Some mediocre doctors may treat it as a dark wind condition. In all blood circulation disorders, heat invades the void, leading to a reversal that affects the heart, thus causing unconsciousness and suffocation. There are three causes: excessive exertion of the heart leading to circulation, significant blood loss, and minor blood loss. Although the symptoms may be similar, treatment varies and should be carefully assessed. For significant blood loss, the symptoms are merely lethargy and agitation; thus, one should use nourishing blood and calming heart medicines. For minor blood loss, there is retained lochia, which obstructs the heart, causing fullness and urgency beneath the heart, with the spirit dimmed and the mouth sealed, resulting in total unawareness; one should use medications to break and promote blood circulation.[11p]

    古法有云：产妇才分娩讫，预烧黄石子，硬炭烧令通赤，置器中，急于床前以酢沃之，得酢气可除血运，产后一月时作为妙。崔氏云：凡运者皆是虚热，血气奔逆，腹中空所致。欲分娩者，第一须先取讝酢以涂口鼻，仍宜酢于旁，使闻其气，兼细细饮之，此为上法。如觉运，则以酢噀面，苏来即饮酢，仍少与解之。一云仍少与水解之。】　　【按郭稽中曰：产后气血暴虚，未得安静，血随气上，迷乱心神，故眼前生花，极甚者令人闷绝不知人，口噤神昏气冷，医者不识，呼为暗风，若作此治之，病必难愈，但服清魂散即省。】[12p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[12p]

    The ancient method states: When a woman has just given birth, one should first heat yellow stone (a type of mineral), burn it into hard charcoal until it turns red, place it in a vessel, and quickly bring it to the bedside alongside vinegar to allow it to absorb the vinegar's vapor, as this can help eliminate blood stagnation. It is best to do this one month after childbirth. Cui suggests that all movement-related conditions are due to vacuous heat and the chaotic energy of blood, resulting from emptiness in the abdomen. For those about to give birth, first, one must apply vinegar to the mouth and nose, and surrounding vinegar should be present to smell, while also consuming it in small sips, which is the best method. If one feels discomfort, vinegar can be sprayed on the face, and upon recovery, one should drink vinegar, or one may also dilute it with a bit of water. According to Guo Jizhong, after childbirth, the vital energy and blood are profoundly depleted, lacking tranquility; thus, the blood rises with the energy, leading to confusion of the mind and often causing vivid hallucinations. In severe cases, this may lead to fainting and an inability to recognize people, with a silent mouth, cloudy mind, and cold energy. If physicians do not recognize this and misdiagnose it as 'dark wind,' treating it as such may result in difficult recovery. Instead, taking a clear spirit powder is sufficient for relief.[12p]

    颠狂

    　　产后颠狂，乃败血上冲，用大圣泽兰散加砂仁末三分，煎酸枣仁汤调下；或朱砂二钱研细，以乳汁调和，入紫项活地龙一条，滚二三沸去之，入酒，再用重汤煮温，分三服。　　【注　前证乃虚神不守舍，非补养元气不可，仍参后各证互用。】　　【按《大全》：产后因惊，败血冲心，昏闷发狂，如有鬼祟，宜用大圣泽兰散，自合者方有效；或煎酸枣仁汤，调好辰砂细末，一服可安。】[13p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[13p]

    Madness After Childbirth

    Madness after childbirth is caused by the upward surging of residual blood. Use a blend of Great Sage Zilan powder mixed with three parts of sand seed, and decoct it with sour jujube seed soup to take it down; or, grind two coins of cinnabar finely, mix with milk, add one live earthworm from Zixiang, boil for two or three minutes and then remove, add wine, and re-boil in strong broth to warm before dividing into three doses.
    [Note: The previous symptoms indicate a false spirit not residing in the body; it is crucial to nourish the essential qi, and one should also refer to the subsequent symptoms for mutual application.]
    [According to the "Comprehensive Guide": After childbirth, due to fright, residual blood surges to the heart, leading to confusion and madness as if beset by spirits; it is advisable to use Great Sage Zilan powder, corresponding with the symptoms for effectiveness; or, decoct sour jujube seed soup and mix with finely ground cinnabar for a single dose to bring calm.][13p]

    狂言讝语

    　　产后狂言讝语，乃心血虚也，用朱砂末酒调下龙虎丹参丸，琥珀地黄丸亦可。　　【注　前证当固胃气为主，而佐以见证之药为善。若一于攻痰则误矣。】　　【按《大全》：产后语言颠倒，或狂言讝语，如见鬼神者，其源不一，须辨证用药治疗。产后惊风，言语乱道，如见鬼神，精神不定者，研好朱砂酒调下龙虎丹参丸三丸作一服，兼琥珀地黄丸服之。产后心虚，败血停积，上干于心，而狂言独语者，当在《良方》乍见鬼神条求之。产后脏虚，心神惊悸，志意不安，语言错乱，不自觉知，神思不安者，当在《良方》惊悸条求之。[14p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[14p]

    Frantic Speech and Strange Words

    Frantic speech and strange words after childbirth arise from a deficiency of heart and blood. This can be treated with a mixture of powdered red arsenic and wine, taken with Longhu Dan Shen Wan, or Amber Di Huang Wan can also be used. [Note: The primary treatment should stabilize the stomach qi, using supporting herbs as necessary. Mistakenly attacking phlegm would lead to error.] [According to "The Great Compendium": After childbirth, when speech becomes confused or frantic, akin to seeing ghosts or spirits, the causes are varied and treatment should be based on careful diagnosis. For postpartum hysteria, with disordered speech resembling sighting ghosts or spirits, and a lack of mental stability, three pills of Longhu Dan Shen Wan mixed with wine should be taken, alongside Amber Di Huang Wan. If there is a deficiency of the internal organs, with a weak heart leading to stagnant blood and chaotic speech, refer to the section on "Seeing Ghosts and Spirits" in "Good Formulas." For postpartum internal deficiency, with heart and spirit fluttering, restless thoughts, confused speech, and unawareness of one’s own state, refer to the section on "Hiccups and Fluttering" in "Good Formulas."][14p]

    宿有风毒，因产心虚气弱，腰背强直，或歌哭嗔笑　言语乱道，当作风证治疗，当在《良方》中风心惊条求之。产后心虚中风，心神恍惚，言语错乱，当在《良方》中风恍惚条求之。产后多因败血迷乱心经而颠狂，言语错乱无常，或运闷者，当于《良方》血运类中求之。产后感冒风寒，恶露斩然不行，憎寒发热如疟，昼日明了，暮则讝语如见鬼状，当作热入血室治之，宜琥珀地黄丸及四物汤，只用生干地黄，如北柴胡等分煎服；如不退者，以小柴胡汤加生干地黄如黄芩分两，煎服自愈。虽然，已上诸证，大抵胎前产后，自有专门一定之法，毫发不同。如产后首当逐败生新，然后仔细详辨疾证，不可妄立名色，自生新意。加减方药，大宜对证。依古法施治，未有不安者也。】[15p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[15p]

    If there is wind affecting the body, it may lead to a weakness of the heart and a deficiency of qi, resulting in rigidity of the waist and back, and disturbances in speech and emotions such as singing, crying, anger, and laughter. This should be treated as wind-related symptoms, and should be sought in the section on heart shock in “Good Prescriptions.” After childbirth, if there is a deficiency in the heart leading to wind, with scattered thoughts and disordered speech, one should look for treatment in the section on wind and confusion in “Good Prescriptions.”

    After childbirth, confusion often occurs due to stagnant blood affecting the heart, resulting in madness and disordered speech. If there is a heavy feeling, one should refer to the section on blood circulation in “Good Prescriptions.”

    If postpartum there is a cold from wind and the lochia does not flow, with a hatred of cold and fever like malaria, clear during the day but manifesting strange speech at night as if seeing ghosts, it should be treated as heat entering the blood chamber, using drugs like Hu Po Di Huang Wan and Si Wu Decoction, employing raw dry Rehmannia like Bei Chai Hu in equal parts for decoction. If symptoms do not subside, one can use Xiao Chai Hu Decoction combined with raw dry Rehmannia and Huang Qin, decocting and taking until recovery.

    Although the above conditions are generally established, before and after childbirth, there are specific methods to treat them and they differ slightly. After childbirth, one should first eliminate the stagnant blood and generate new blood, then carefully distinguish the symptoms without arbitrarily naming them or generating new ideas. The modifications to prescriptions should be well-suited to the symptoms. Following ancient methods for treatment, there have been no discomforts encountered.[15p]

    不语

    　　产后不语，因心气虚而不能通于舌，则舌强不能言语者，宜服七珍散。余当推其所因而治之可也。　　【注　经云：大肠之脉散舌下。又云：脾之脉，是动则病舌本强不能言。又云：肾之别脉上入于心，系舌本，虚则不能言。窃谓前证，若心肾气虚，用七珍散。肾虚风热，地黄饮。大肠风热，加味逍遥散加防风、白芷。脾经风热，秦艽升麻汤。肝经风热，柴胡清肝散加防风、白芷。脾气郁结，加味归脾汤加升麻。肝木太过，小柴胡加钓藤钩。脾土受侮，六君加升麻、白芷、钓藤钩。肝脾血虚，用佛手散。脾气虚，用四君子。气血俱虚，八珍汤；如不应，用独参汤；更不应，急加附子补其气而生其血。若竟月血药则误矣。】　　【按《大全》：人心有七孔三毛，产后虚弱，多致停积败血，闭于心窍，神志不能明了；又心气通于舌，心气闭塞则舌亦强矣，故令不语。】[16p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[16p]

    Silence

    After childbirth, if one does not speak, it is due to the deficiency of heart energy, which cannot connect to the tongue. In cases where the tongue is strong but one cannot speak, it is advisable to take the Seven Treasures Powder. I will analyze the cause and treat accordingly.

    Notes: The text states that the meridian of the large intestine disperses under the tongue. It also mentions that if the spleen's meridian is active, it can lead to a tongue that appears strong but cannot speak. Furthermore, the kidney's collateral meridian ascends to the heart, connecting to the tongue; when weakened, one is unable to speak. I propose that for the aforementioned condition, if heart and kidney energy is deficient, the Seven Treasures Powder should be used. For kidney deficiency with wind-heat, use Rehmannia Decoction. For large intestine wind-heat, add modified Xiao Yao San with Fang Feng and Bai Zhi. For spleen meridian wind-heat, use Qin Jiao Sheng Ma Decoction. For liver meridian wind-heat, use Chai Hu Qing Gan San with Fang Feng and Bai Zhi. For spleen energy stagnation, add modified Gui Pi Decoction with Sheng Ma. If liver wood is excessive, use Xiao Chai Hu with Dai Tong Gou. If the spleen earth has been insulted, modify Liu Jun Zi with Sheng Ma, Bai Zhi, and Dai Tong Gou. For deficiency of liver and spleen blood, use Fo Shou San. For spleen energy deficiency, use Si Jun Zi. If both energy and blood are deficient, use Ba Zhen Tang. If that does not respond, use Du Shen Tang. If still unresponsive, urgently add Fu Zi to replenish energy and nourish blood. It would be a mistake to continue with blood-tonifying herbs for a month.

    Commentary: According to "Da Quan," the human heart has seven openings and three pores. After childbirth, weakness often leads to stagnation of blood and waste, obstructing the heart orifices, causing confusion in spirit and mind. Since heart energy connects to the tongue, if it is blocked, the tongue becomes strong yet mute, hence the silence.[16p]

    乍见鬼神

    　　产后如见鬼神，或言语讝妄，皆由血气损亏，阴虚发热，或瘀血停滞，以致心神烦躁而然也，宜以调经散治之。　　【注　前证若败血停滞，用调经散；若血虚发热，用八珍加炮姜；若心血虚损，用柏子仁散。大抵此证皆心脾血少所致，但调补胃气，则痰清而神自安矣。若果系鬼祟所附，即灸鬼哭穴可愈。其或不起者，多因豁痰降火攻伐之过也。】　　【按《大全》：心主身之血脉，因产伤耗血脉，心气虚则败血停积，上干于心，心不受触，遂致心中烦躁，卧起不安，乍见鬼神，言语颠倒错妄。医人不识，呼为风邪。如此治之，必不得愈。但服调经散，每服加龙脑一捻，得睡即安。】　　【按郭氏云：肝藏血，心主血。因产走耗其血，劳动肝心，败血奔冲，邪淫于心，所以乍见鬼神，言语颠倒，非风邪也。但服调经散，加生龙脑一捻，煎服得睡即安。黑龙丹亦能治疗。】[17p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[17p]

    Encountering Ghosts and Spirits

    After childbirth, if one feels as though they see ghosts and spirits, or speaks erratically, it is due to a deficiency in blood and energy, leading to yin deficiency and internal heat, or stagnation of blood. This results in anxiety and agitation of the mind, and should be treated with a regulating menstruation powder.

    (Note: If the symptoms are due to stagnant blood, use regulating menstruation powder; if due to blood deficiency and heat, use Eight Treasures decoction with roasted ginger; if due to heart blood deficiency, use Bai Zi Ren powder. Generally, these symptoms arise from insufficient heart and spleen blood. However, if the stomach energy is balanced, phlegm will clear, and the spirit will find peace. If indeed it is due to a ghostly presence, moxibustion at the "ghost weeping point" can cure it. Failure to achieve results often stems from excessive phlegm clearing and fire attacking the heart.)

    According to the "Great Compendium": The heart governs the blood arteries. Blood is consumed and depleted due to childbirth, and the energy of the heart becomes weak. As a result, stagnant blood accumulates, affecting the heart, and the heart does not respond, leading to restlessness, inability to sleep, and sudden encounters with ghosts and spirits, causing nonsensical speech. If medical practitioners do not recognize this, they mistakenly diagnose it as wind evil. Treating it this way will surely not lead to recovery. However, taking regulating menstruation powder—each time, add a pinch of dragon brain—can induce sleep and stabilize the condition.

    Furthermore, according to Guo's assertion: The liver stores blood, while the heart governs blood. Due to childbirth, blood is depleted, stressing the liver and heart, causing chaotic blood flow and evil afflicting the heart, hence the experience of seeing ghosts and spirits and speaking erratically, not due to wind evil. Again, taking regulating menstruation powder with a pinch of fresh dragon brain can ensure peaceful sleep. The Black Dragon Pill can also serve as treatment.[17p]

    惊悸

    　　产后心神惊悸恐惧，或目睛不转，口不能言，乃心气虚而六淫内侵，诊其脉动而弱者惊悸也。动则为惊，弱则为悸矣。　　【注　人之所主者心，心之所主者血，心血一虚，神气不守，此惊悸所由作也，当补血气为主。】　　【按《大全》：产后脏虚心神惊悸者，由体虚心气不足，心之经为风邪所乘也。或恐惧忧迫，令心气受于风邪，邪搏于心，则惊不自安，若惊不已，则悸动不定，其状目睛不转而不能动。】[18p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[18p]

    Palpitations

    After childbirth, one may experience sudden fright and fear, or the eyes may not move, and the mouth cannot speak. This is due to the deficiency of heart qi and the internal invasion of the six evils. When the pulse is weak, it indicates palpitations. Movement induces fright, and weakness leads to palpitations.

    (Note: The heart governs the spirit, and the heart is governed by blood. When both heart and blood are deficient, the spirit cannot be secured, which leads to palpitations. It is essential to supplement blood and qi as the main treatment.)

    (Observation from "Great Compendium": Palpitations after childbirth due to organ deficiency and a startled spirit arise from a weak body with insufficient heart qi. The heart's channel may be invaded by wind evil. Fear and anxiety may cause heart qi to be affected by wind evil so that the spirit is unsettled. If the fright persists, the palpitations become irregular, causing the eyes not to move and the person unable to act.)[18p]

    心神恍惚

    　　产后恍惚，因元气俱虚，心经血少，或外邪所侵，以致心神恍惚，怔忡不宁。　　【注　前证当大补血气为主，而佐以诸方为善，盖风为虚极之假象也。固其本源，诸病自退，若专治风则速其危矣。】　　【按《大全》：心主血，血气通于荣卫脏腑，遍循经络。产则血气俱伤，五脏皆虚，荣卫不足，即为风邪所乘，则令心神恍惚不定也。】
    　　　　　虚极生风

    　　产后生风，因去血过多，气无所主，以致唇青肉冷汗出，目眩神昏，命在须臾，此但虚极生风也，急服济危上丹。若投以风药则误甚矣。　　【注　前证若心脾血气俱虚，用十全大补汤；如不应，加附子钓藤钩。若肝经血虚，用逍遥散加钓藤。经云：脾之荣在唇，心之液为汗。若心脾二脏虚极，急用参附汤救之。】[19p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[19p]

    Mental Confusion

    Postpartum confusion arises when the vital energy is exhausted, causing a deficiency in heart blood. This may also result from external pathogens invading the body, leading to a disordered and unsettled mind. [Note: In this case, the primary treatment should focus on boosting blood and energy, supported by various remedies, as the wind is merely a false appearance of extreme deficiency. By addressing the root cause, all other ailments will retreat; focusing solely on the wind could exacerbate the crisis.] [According to the "Comprehensive Manual": the heart governs blood, and blood and energy circulate through the nourishing and defensive organs, spreading along the meridians. After childbirth, blood and energy are both harmed, leaving the five organs deficient, with inadequate nourishment to resist wind pathogens, which disrupts the mind.]

    Extreme Deficiency Causes Wind

    Postpartum wind arises from excessive blood loss, leaving the energy without a host, resulting in symptoms such as blue lips, cold flesh, sweating, blurred vision, and fainting—indicating imminent danger. This is purely the result of extreme deficiency causing wind; immediate administration of the “Emergency Elixir” is necessary. Using wind remedies would be a grave mistake. [Note: In this case, if both heart and spleen are deficient in blood and energy, one should use the "Ten Complete Tonifying Decoction"; if ineffective, add aconite and vine bitters. If liver channel blood is deficient, use "Happy Remedy" with vine bitters. It is said: "The nourishment of the spleen is reflected in the lips, and the essence of the heart is sweat." When both the heart and spleen are extremely deficient, urgently use “Ginseng and Aconite Decoction” to rescue the patient.][19p]

    虚汗不止

    　　产后汗不止者，皆由阳气顿虚，腠理不密，而津液妄泄也。若遇风则变痉，纵不成痉，亦虚乏短气，身体消瘦，唇口干燥，久则经水断绝，由津液竭故也。　　【注　前证属血气俱虚，急用十全大补汤；如不应，用参附、芪附等汤。若汗多亡阳发痉，尤当用前药。王海藏先生云：头汗出至颈而还，额上偏多，盖额为六阳之所会也，由虚热熏蒸而出。窃谓前证，当以部位分之，额左属肝，额右属肺，鼻属脾，颐属肾，额属心。治者审之。】　　【按《大全》：虚汗不止者，由阴气虚而阳气加之，里虚表实，阳气独发于外，故汗出也。血为阴，产则伤血，是为阴气虚也。气为阳，其气实者，阳加于阴，故令汗出，而阴气虚弱不复者，则汗出不止也。凡产后血气皆虚，故多汗。】[20p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[20p]

    Continuous Sweating

    If sweating does not stop after childbirth, it is due to a sudden deficiency of Yang energy, leading to loose pores and the disorderly leakage of bodily fluids. If exposed to wind, it may lead to spasms; even if spasms do not occur, there will be a sense of weakness and shortness of breath, with the body becoming emaciated and the lips and mouth dry. Over time, this may result in the cessation of menstruation due to the depletion of fluids.

    [Notes: The condition concerns both blood and energy deficiency; immediate use of the "Shiquan Dabu Decoction" is advised. If ineffective, decoctions such as "Shenfu" and "Qifu" should be used. Mr. Wang Haizang mentions that sweat from the head reaching the neck and being more pronounced on the forehead indicates that the forehead is where the six Yangs converge, resulting from the evaporation of deficient heat. I believe this condition should be categorized by location: the left forehead corresponds to the liver, the right to the lungs, the nose to the spleen, the jaw to the kidneys, and the forehead itself to the heart. Practitioners should examine this carefully.]

    [According to "The Great Compendium": Continuous sweating is caused by a deficiency of Yin energy combined with an excess of Yang energy, leading to internal deficiency and external excess, where Yang energy is primarily expelled outward, thus causing sweating. Blood is Yin, and childbirth injures blood, resulting in a deficiency of Yin energy. Qi is Yang; when the Qi is sufficient, Yang is added to Yin, causing sweating, while if Yin energy is weak and cannot recover, sweating will persist. Generally, after childbirth, both blood and energy are deficient, thus leading to excessive sweating.][20p]

    冒闷汗出

    　　产后忽冒闷汗出者，因阴血亏损，阳气郁暴故也，急吞鸡子三枚；未醒，饮童便。或时复发，宜服竹沥五合。　　【注　前证属大虚，宜固元气鸟主。其汗不止，必变柔痉。东垣先生云：妇人分娩及半产漏下，昏冒目瞑，盖因血暴亡而火上炽，但补其血则神自昌。若常时血下，当补而升举其气，阳得血而神安则目明矣。今立一方，以补手足厥阴之血，兼益阳气，名曰全生活血汤。】[21p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[21p]

    If postpartum excessive sweating occurs suddenly, it is due to the deficiency of yin blood and the excessive accumulation of yang qi. Quickly consume three eggs; if not yet awake, drink children's urine. If it recurs, it is advisable to take five doses of bamboo juice.

    (Note: The previous symptoms indicate great deficiency, and one should consolidate the vital energy. If sweating does not stop, it may turn into soft convulsions. Mr. Dongyuan states that for women in labor or experiencing postpartum bleeding, with fainting and closed eyes, this is due to a drastic loss of blood leading to excessive fire. However, replenishing the blood will naturally restore the spirit. If there is frequent bleeding, one should nourish and lift the qi; with blood nourishing the yang, the spirit will be settled, and vision will return. A formula is established to replenish the blood of both the hands and feet's shaoyin and to also boost yang qi, named the Complete Life-Blood Decoction.)[21p]

    汗多变痉

    　　产后汗多变痉，因气血亏损，肉理不密，风邪所乘，其形口噤背强如癎，或摇头马嘶，身反折，不时举发，气息如绝，宜速灌小续命汤。若汗出如雨，手拭不及者不治。　　【注　前证因去血过多，元气亏极，或外邪相搏，以致牙关紧急，四肢痉强，或阴火内动，或腰背反张，肢体抽搐，若有汗而不恶寒者名柔痉，若无汗而恶寒者曰刚痉。由亡血过多，筋无所养。故伤寒汗下过多，溃疡脓血大泄，多患之，乃败证也，急以十全大补汤治之。如不应急加附子，多有复苏者。亦有六淫七情所致者。】　　【按《大全》：产后发痉，汗出如雨，手摸空者，不可治也。按郭氏曰：产后血虚肉理不密故多汗，因遇风邪搏之则变痓也。】[22p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[22p]

    After childbirth, excessive sweating can lead to convulsions due to the loss of vital energy and blood, resulting in loose flesh. Wind pathogens invade the body, causing rigidity in the jaw and back, resembling seizures. Symptoms may include shaking of the head and body, odd postures, and gasping for breath. It is advisable to quickly administer a small soup for reviving life. If sweating is profuse and one cannot wipe their hands dry, treatment is ineffective.

    (Note: The previous condition is caused by excessive blood loss, leading to severe deficiency of vital energy, or by external pathogens clashing, resulting in tightness in the jaw and stiffness in the limbs. There may also be internal heat from Yin, or the back may arch, causing spasms in the limbs. If there is sweating without aversion to cold, it is termed "soft convulsion"; if there is no sweating but a fear of cold, it is termed "rigid convulsion." The excessive loss of blood results in insufficient nourishment for the tendons. Thus, excessive sweating and purging in cases of infectious fever, along with large discharges of pus and blood, can lead to severe illness and should be treated urgently with a comprehensive tonic. If there is no response, adding Fuzi may resuscitate many. There may also be conditions caused by the six excesses or the seven emotions.)

    (Note: According to "The Great Compendium," convulsions occurring after childbirth, with sweating resembling rain and hands feeling empty, should not be treated. Guo states that postpartum blood deficiency and loose flesh lead to excessive sweating, which, when combined with wind pathogens, results in conversion of the condition.)[22p]

    中风

    　　产后中风，或血气未复，风寒所感，以致筋挛拘急，口眼喎斜，或肢体缓弱，入脏则恍惚惊悸。郭稽中云：产后强力下床，或误入房，或忧怒内伤脏脏。陈无择曰：当以脉辨而治之。若努力下床，月内入房，忧怒着急，非中风类，乃蓐劳火邪之证也。　　【注　前证果外邪所属，形气不足，病气有余，当补元气为主，稍佐以治病之药。若强力下床，月内入房，属形气病气俱不足，当纯补元气，多有复苏者。若误投风药，乃促其危也。[23p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[23p]

    Stroke

    Postpartum stroke can occur due to inadequate recovery of blood and qi, or due to being affected by wind and cold, leading to muscle spasms and rigidity, facial asymmetry, or weakness in limbs. When it affects the internal organs, it may cause confusion and palpitations. Guo Jizhong states that a strong effort to get out of bed after childbirth, or mistakenly engaging in sexual activity, or being disturbed by worry or anger can harm the internal organs. Chen Wuze suggests treatment should be based on pulse diagnosis. If one forces getting out of bed, engages in sexual activity within a month, or experiences worry and anxiety, it is not a case of stroke, but rather a manifestation of excess fire from overexertion.

    Note: In the prior symptoms, external pathogens are related, with insufficient essence and qi present, leading to an excess of pathogenic energy. The main approach should be to tonify the original qi, with some assistance from medicinal treatments. If there is a strong effort to get out of bed and engage in sexual activity within the month, it suggests that both essence and qi are insufficient, thus pure tonification of the original qi is necessary, with many cases showing recovery. Mistakenly using stroke medicine would further endanger the situation.[23p]

    】　　【按《大全》：产时伤动血气，劳损脏腑，未曾平复，起早劳动，致使气虚，而风邪乘虚入之，故中风。风邪冷气，客于皮肤经络，但疼痹，羸乏不任，少气。大凡筋脉挟寒则挛急喎僻，挟温则纵缓虚弱。若入诸脏，恍惚惊悸，随其所伤脏腑经络而生病焉。】　　【按郭嵇中曰：产后五七日内，强力下床，或一月之内，伤于房室，或怀忧怒扰荡冲和，或因食生硬，伤动脏腑，得病之初，眼濇口噤，肌肉润搐，渐至腰脊筋急强直者，不可治。此乃人作，非偶尔中风所得也。】[24p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[24p]

    According to the "Comprehensive Canon": During childbirth, if one strains the blood and vital energy, damages the internal organs, and has not yet recovered, getting up early to work can lead to qi deficiency. This allows wind pathogens to enter. Wind pathogens, coupled with cold qi, invade the skin and meridians, causing pain and numbness, weakness, and shortness of breath. Generally, if the tendons and channels are affected by cold, they will contract and spasm; if affected by warmth, they become loose and weak. If such conditions invade the internal organs, symptoms like confusion and palpitations will occur, depending on which organs and channels are harmed.

    According to Guo Jizhong: Within five to seven days after childbirth, if one forces themselves to get out of bed or within a month, if there is damage due to excessive exertion, emotional distress from worry or anger, or due to eating hard foods which harm the internal organs, symptoms may initially appear as dry eyes, unable to speak, and muscular twitching, gradually leading to a stiff and tense waist and spine. This should not be treated; it is caused by one's own actions, not a spontaneous occurrence of wind stroke.[24p]

    口噤腰背反张

    　　产后口噤，由血气虚而风邪乘于手三阳经也。盖手三阳之筋循结于颔，得风冷则筋急。故致口噤，腰背挛急，角弓反张者，是风邪入于诸阳之经也。　　【注　前证因气血耗损，腠理不密，汗出过多而患之者，乃虚象也，宜固气血为主，佐以本方。丹溪云：产后当大补气血为先，虽有他证，以末治之。如恶寒发热等证，乃气血虚损之极也，宜大剂参、芪、归、朮、肉桂以培养之；如不应，急用炮附子；再不应，用人参一两，炮附子二三钱，名参附汤。[25p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[25p]

    Mouth Muteness and Arched Back Postpartum

    Mouth muteness after childbirth is caused by deficiency of blood and qi, allowing wind evil to invade the hand's three yang channels. The tendons of the three yang channels converge at the jaw; when exposed to cold wind, these tendons become tense. Therefore, the resulting mouth muteness and the arching and tightening of the waist and back occur because the wind evil enters the channels of the yang.

    [Note: The preceding symptoms are due to the depletion of qi and blood, with the pores being open, causing excessive sweating and leading to this condition, which is a manifestation of deficiency. It is advisable to focus on consolidating qi and blood as the primary treatment, supplemented with this formula. Danxi states that postpartum, one should first greatly replenish qi and blood. Even if there are other symptoms, they should be treated as secondary. Symptoms like aversion to cold and fever indicate extreme deficiency of qi and blood, and one should use a large dose of ginseng, astragalus, angelica, poria, and cinnamon to nourish. If there is no response, swiftly use processed aconite; if still ineffective, take one ounce of ginseng and two to three qian of processed aconite, known as Ginseng and Aconite Decoction.][25p]

    设犹未应，乃药力未能及也，宜多用之。】　　产后眼张口噤，肢体强直，腰背反偃，言语错乱如癎者，此气虚风邪所伤而成痉也。　　【注　按仲景先生云：有汗为柔痉，用桂枝汤；无汗为刚痉，用麻黄汤。然产后得此，血气俱虚之败证也，不可与伤寒例看。丹溪先生云：产后当大补气血为主，可用十全大补汤以补元气；如不应，急加附子。更不应，是药力弗逮也，仍用参附汤多服。余尝治大虚之证，参芪数斤、附子数枚方应。】　　【按《大全》：宿有风毒，因产心气虚弱，发成风痓，心闷气绝。】
    　　　　　筋挛

    　　产后中风，筋脉挛急，乃血气俱虚。或风邪客于皮肤则顽痹嬴乏，若入于筋脉则四肢挛急，皆由大经空虚，风寒乘虚而渐入也。　　【注　肝属木而主筋。前证若肝经风热血燥，用加味逍遥散，如不应，当用六味地黄丸以补肾水。经云：风客淫气，精乃亡，邪伤肝也。仍参杂证诸血风方论治之。】[26p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[26p]

    If the response is still not adequate, then the medicinal effect has not reached its target; it is advisable to use it more. After childbirth, if the eyes are wide open and the mouth is sealed, with rigid limbs, a bent back, and incoherent speech similar to convulsions, this condition is caused by deficiency of qi and invasion of wind leading to spasms.

    According to Mr. Zhongjing, in cases of soft spasms with sweating, one should use Gui Zhi Decoction; if there is no sweating, it indicates stiff spasms, warranting the use of Ma Huang Decoction. However, in postpartum situations, both blood and qi are deficient, and this should not be viewed under the same criteria as for febrile diseases. Mr. Danxi states that after childbirth, it is crucial to significantly replenish qi and blood, recommending the use of Shi Quan Da Bu Decoction to restore the original qi; if this is ineffective, one should urgently add Fu Zi. If there still is no response, it suggests that the medicinal effect has not been sufficient, and one should continue taking Ren Fu Decoction in larger quantities. I have previously treated cases of great deficiency using several pounds of Ren Shen and a few pieces of Fu Zi as necessary.

    Furthermore, according to "The Complete Guide," if there is lingering wind toxin and the heart qi is weak after childbirth, it may develop into wind stroke, leading to chest tightness and cessation of qi flow.

    Muscle spasms

    Postpartum wind can cause muscle and tendon rigidity due to the deficiency of blood and qi. If the wind pathogen invades the skin, it may result in stubborn fatigue; if it penetrates the tendons and vessels, all four limbs may become rigid. This is due to the great channels being empty, allowing wind and cold to gradually enter.

    Note that the liver corresponds to wood and governs tendons. In prior symptoms where the liver channel displays wind-heat and dry blood, one should use Jia Wei Xiao Yao San. If this is ineffective, then Liu Wei Di Huang Wan should be employed to tonify kidney water. The scripture states: "Wind invades the excessive humidity, thus essence is lost; the pathogen harms the liver." One should also consider mixing symptoms with treatments focused on blood and wind disorders.[26p]

    瘈瘲

    　　窃谓：瘈者，筋脉拘急也；瘲者，筋脉张纵也。经云：肝主筋而藏血。盖肝气为阳为火，肝血为阴为水，前证因产后阴血去多，阳火炽盛，筋无所养而然耳。故痈疽脓水过多，金疮出血过甚，则阳随阴散，亦多致此。治法当用八珍散加丹皮、钓藤以生阴血，则阳火自退，诸证自愈；如不应，当用四君、芎、归、丹皮、钓藤以补脾土。盖血生于至阴，至阴者脾土也。故小儿吐泻之后，脾胃亏损亦多患之，乃虚象也，无风可逐，无痰可消。若属阳气脱陷者，用补中益气加姜、桂；阴气虚败者，用十全大补加桂、附，亦有复生者。此等证候，若肢体恶寒脉微细者，此为真状；若脉浮大发热烦渴，此为假象。惟当固本为善。若无力抽搐，戴眼反折，汗出如珠流者，皆不治。[27p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[27p]

    Convulsions

    I humbly propose that "convulsions" refer to the tightening of the tendons and channels; "twitching" refers to their stretching and relaxing. The classics state: the liver governs the tendons and stores blood. Liver qi is yang and fire, while liver blood is yin and water. The previous condition stems from excessive loss of yin blood after childbirth, leading to excessive yang fire, which causes the tendons to lack nourishment. Hence, when there is excessive pus and water from sores, or excessive bleeding from wounds, the yang disperses with the yin, also leading to this condition.

    The treatment principle should employ Eight Treasures Powder, adding Dan Pi and Tian Ma to nourish yin blood, allowing yang fire to recede and the various symptoms to resolve; if there is no response, one should use Four Gentlemen Decoction, with Chuanxiong, Angelica, Dan Pi, and Tian Ma to supplement the spleen. Blood is produced from the most yin, and the most yin is the spleen’s earth. Therefore, after vomiting and diarrhea in children, spleen and stomach deficiency is common; this is a virtual symptom, without wind to expel or phlegm to dissipate. If it pertains to the collapse of yang qi, one should use the Restore the Middle and Augment Qi Decoction with ginger and cinnamon; if it is due to failure of yin qi, use the Complete Great Tonifying Decoction with cinnamon and Aconite, which may also lead to recovery.

    In these cases, if there are chills in the limbs and a thin pulse, this is the true condition; if the pulse is floating, large, with fever and thirst, this is a false condition. One should steadfastly stabilize the fundamental aspects. If there's loss of strength with convulsions, bending of the eyes, or sweating like flowing pearls, none should be treated.[27p]

    脚气

    　　产后脚气，因元气内虚，六淫外侵所致，其状发热，瞀闷搐掣，惊悸心烦，呕吐气上，用小续命汤治之，若以为败血攻之则误矣。　　【注　前证当补气血为主，佐以小续命汤、寄生汤；如不应，用大防风汤。仍参脚气方论主之。】　　【按《大全》：产后热闷气上，转为脚气者何？答曰：产后血虚主热，复因春夏取凉过多，地之蒸湿，因足履之，所以着为脚气，可服小续命汤二三剂，必愈。若医者误以败血药攻之，则血去而病益增矣。】　　【按郭氏云：恶附子者，宜服独活寄生汤；若呕者，去地黄倍加生姜。】[28p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[28p]

    Athlete's Foot

    Postpartum athlete's foot occurs due to internal deficiency of vital energy and external invasion of the six excesses. Symptoms include heat, confusion, twitching, anxiety, restlessness, and regurgitation. It should be treated with the Xiao Xuming Decoction; using blood-attack methods would be a mistake.

    [Note: For the condition, the main focus should be on replenishing qi and blood, supplemented with Xiao Xuming Decoction and Jisheng Decoction; if ineffective, use Da Fengfeng Decoction. Refer to the Athletes' Foot formula for guidance.]

    [According to the "Great Compendium": Why does postpartum heat and anxiety transition into athlete's foot? The answer is: postpartum blood deficiency leads to heat, and excessive cooling during spring and summer, combined with dampness from the ground affecting the feet, results in athlete's foot. Taking Xiao Xuming Decoction for two or three doses will certainly cure it. If the physician mistakenly uses blood-attack medicine, it will deplete the blood and worsen the condition.]

    [According to Guo's commentary: For those who cannot tolerate Aconite, it is advisable to take Du Huo Jisheng Decoction; if nausea occurs, remove Rehmannia and double the ginger.][28p]

    遍身疼痛

    　　产后遍身疼痛者，由气虚百节开张，血流骨节，以致肢体沉重不利，筋脉引急，发热头痛，宜用趁痛散治之。陈无择云：若兼感寒伤食，宜用五积散。若误作伤寒发汗，则筋脉抽搐，手足厥冷，则变为痉，当大补气血为主。　　【注　前证若以手按而痛益甚，是血瘀滞也，用四物、炮姜、红花、桃仁、泽兰补而散之。若按而痛稍缓，此是血虚也，用四物、炮姜、人参、白朮补而养之。】　　【按《大全》：产后气弱，经络肉分之间，血多留滞，累日不散，则骨节不利，筋脉急引，故腰背不得转侧，手足不能动摇，身热头痛也。若医以为伤寒治之，则汗出而筋脉动惕，手足厥冷，变生他病，但服趁痛散除之。】　　【按郭氏云：因产走动，血气升降，失其常度，留滞关节，筋脉引急，是以遍身疼痛，其则腰背强硬，不能俯仰，手足拘挛不能伸屈，或身热头痛，不可作他病治，但服趁痛散循流血气，使筋脉舒畅，疼痛自止。】[29p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[29p]

    Widespread Pain After Childbirth

    Postpartum widespread pain is caused by Qi deficiency leading to the expansion of the joints and stagnation of blood in the bones, resulting in heavy limbs and restricted movement, accompanied by muscle tension, fever, and headache. It is advisable to use Chen Tong San for treatment. Chen Wuze states that if there are symptoms of cold injury after eating, Wu Ji San should be employed. Should one mistakenly treat it as a cold and induce sweating, it may lead to muscle spasms and coldness in the hands and feet, which can turn into convulsions; thus, it is crucial to prioritize the restoration of Qi and blood.

    Note: If pressing on the affected area increases the pain, this indicates blood stagnation; using Si Wu, Pao Jiang, Hong Hua, Tao Ren, and Ze Lan can help restore and disperse it. If pressing eases the pain slightly, this points to blood deficiency; employing Si Wu, Pao Jiang, Ren Shen, and Bai Zhu can nourish and support it.

    According to the "Great Compendium": After childbirth, with weakened Qi and blockage within the meridians, if blood stagnation persists over several days, joint movement becomes difficult, and muscles become tense, leading to an inability to turn or move the back and limbs, as well as fever and headache. A mistaken diagnosis of cold may lead to sweating treatments resulting in chaotic muscle movement and cold extremities, causing further complications; hence, taking Chen Tong San can alleviate this.

    Regarding Guo’s explanation: Postpartum activity and disruption of the blood and Qi balance can cause stagnation at the joints and muscle tension, leading to widespread pain. Symptoms include rigidity in the lower back and inability to bend or extend limbs, possibly accompanied by fever and headache. It is not advisable to treat it as another illness; instead, using Chen Tong San to enhance blood and Qi flow will relieve the pain spontaneously.[29p]

    腰痛

    　　肾主腰脚，产后腰痛者，盖肾为胞胎所系，因此产劳伤肾气，以致风冷客之。若连背脊痛久未已，后遇有娠，必致损动。　　【注　前证真气虚邪乘之者，用当归黄芪汤或十全大补为主，佐以寄生汤；如不应，用十全大补加附子。】
    　　　　　恶露不绝

    　　产后恶露不绝，因伤经血，或因有冷气，而脏腑不调故也。　　【注　前证若肝气热而不能生血，用六味地黄丸。若肝气虚而不能藏血，用逍遥散。若脾气虚而不能摄血，用六君子汤。胃气下陷而不能统血，用补中益气汤。若脾经郁热而血不归源，用加味归脾汤。若肝经怒火而血妄行，用加味四物汤。若气血俱虚，用十全大补汤。若肝经风邪而血沸腾，用一味防风丸。】　　【按《大全》：产后恶露不绝者，由产后伤于经血虚损不足，或分解之时，恶血不尽，在于腹中，而脏腑挟于宿冷，致气血不调，故令恶露淋沥不绝也。】
    　　　　　恶露不下[30p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[30p]

    Lower Back Pain

    The kidneys govern the lower back and feet. Those who experience lower back pain after childbirth do so because the kidneys are associated with the womb. Labor depletes kidney energy, leading to the invasion of wind and cold. If back pain persists for a long time, it can provoke complications when conceiving again.
    [Note: For true Qi deficiency with pathogenic factors, use Dong Quai and Astragalus Decoction or the Complete Tonifying Decoction as the main treatment, supplemented with the Parasitic Herbal Decoction; if ineffective, use the Complete Tonifying Decoction with Aconite.]

    Continuous Lochia

    Continuous lochia after childbirth is due to damage to the menstrual blood or the presence of cold air, resulting in dysfunction of the internal organs.
    [Note: If there is liver heat preventing blood formation, use the Six-Flavor Rehmannia Pill. If there is liver Qi deficiency unable to store blood, use the Happy Wanderer Powder. If there is spleen Qi deficiency unable to contain blood, use the Six Gentlemen Decoction. If stomach Qi is descending and cannot regulate blood, use the Middle Supplementing and Qi Benefiting Decoction. If there is damp heat in the spleen channel and blood does not return to its source, use the Modified Gui Pi Decoction. If there is liver channel anger causing blood to flow erratically, use the Modified Four Substance Decoction. If both Qi and blood are deficient, use the Complete Tonifying Decoction. If there is wind evil in the liver channel causing blood tumult, use a single Fang Feng Pill.]
    [According to "The Complete Encyclopedia": Continuous lochia after childbirth results from injury to the menstrual blood, leading to insufficiency, or because the lochia has not completely expelled during delivery, remaining in the abdomen. The internal organs may be burdened by retained cold, causing a disturbance in Qi and blood, which leads to continuous bleeding of lochia.]

    Lochia Not Discharging[30p]

    产后恶露不下，因脏腑劳伤，气血虚损，或风冷相搏所致。　　【注　前证若恶露不下，用失笑散。若气滞血凝，用花蕊石散。若因去血过多而烦热不止，宜参产后儿枕腹痛方论主治。】　　【按《大全》：恶露不下者，由产后脏腑劳伤，气血虚损，或胞络挟于宿冷，或产后当风取凉，风冷乘虚而搏于血，血则壅滞不宣，积蓄在内，故令恶露不下也。】
    　　　　　恶露上攻心痛

    　　产后心痛，为阴血亏损，随火上冲心络，名曰心胞络痛，宜大岩蜜汤治之。若寒伤心经，名曰真心痛，朝发夕死，夕发朝死，无药可救。　　【注　前证若阳气虚寒，用岩蜜汤温之。瘀血上冲，用失笑散散之。血既散而痛仍作，用八珍汤补之。大凡肚腹作痛。以手按之却不痛，此血虚也，须用补养之剂。】
    　　　　　腹痛

    　　产后腹痛，或因外感五邪，内伤六淫，或瘀血壅滞所致，当审其因而治之。　　【注　前证若瘀血内停，用失笑散。风寒外感，用五积散。】[31p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[31p]

    After childbirth, if the lochia does not discharge, it may be due to injury to the internal organs, deficiency of qi and blood, or an attack from wind and cold. If lochia does not discharge due to these symptoms, use Shixiao Powder. If due to qi stagnation and blood clots, use Huairuishi Powder. If excessive blood loss leads to persistent heat, refer to the treatment for abdominal pain after childbirth.

    Lochia not discharging results from injury to the internal organs post-delivery, deficiency of qi and blood, or residual cold retained in the uterine vessels. Alternately, exposure to cold winds post-delivery may cause cold to invade and hinder the blood, leading to stagnation and accumulation, thus preventing the discharge of lochia.

    Regarding postpartum heart pain, it arises from the deficiency of yin blood, which allows heat to ascend and impact the heart vessels, termed heart vessel pain, treated with Dayan Honey Decoction. If cold injures the heart, this is termed true heart pain, which can be fatal within the day. No remedy can help if such a condition arises.

    In cases of postpartum abdominal pain, it may stem from external pathogens or internal injuries, including accumulation of blood clots, requiring careful diagnosis and treatment. If due to stagnant blood inside, use Shixiao Powder; if from cold wind externally, use Wujisan.[31p]

    儿枕腹痛

    　　产后儿枕者，乃母胎中宿血也，或因风冷凝滞于小腹而作痛。　　【注　前证若宿血作痛，失笑散行之；既散而仍痛，四神散调之。若恶心作呕，此属气虚，用六君子健其胃气。若发热头痛，或腹痛按而不痛，此属血虚，用四物、炮姜、参、朮补其脾气。】　　【按《大全》：儿枕者，由母胎中宿有血块，因产时其血破散，与儿俱下则无患也。若产妇脏腑风冷，使血凝滞在于小腹不能流通，则令结聚疼痛，名之曰儿枕也。】
    　　　　　小腹痛

    　　产后小腹作痛，由恶露凝结，或外寒相搏，若久而不散，必成血瘕，而月水不调。　　【注　前证若因气滞，用延胡索散。若因外寒，用五积散。若因怒气，用四物加木香、柴胡。若因血虚，用四物、参、朮、炮姜。若因阳气虚弱，用四君、当归、炮姜。若因脾虚血弱，用六君、当归、炮姜治之。】[32p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[32p]

    Child's Fontanelle Pain

    For a child experiencing pain in the fontanelle after birth, it is caused by retained blood from the mother's womb, or possibly due to cold wind causing blockage in the lower abdomen. If the pain is due to retained blood, disperse it with Xie Xiao San; if the pain persists after dispersal, use Si Shen San to regulate it. If there are symptoms of nausea or vomiting, it indicates Qi deficiency, treat with Liu Jun Zi to strengthen the stomach Qi. If there is fever and headache, or abdominal pain that does not cause agony when pressed, it indicates blood deficiency; use Si Wu, Pao Jiang, Ren Shen, and Bai Zhu to nourish the spleen Qi.

    Notes from "Da Quan": The child’s fontanelle pain is due to a blood clot retained in the mother’s womb. If the blood breaks apart during labor and flows down with the child, there is no issue. If the mother's organs are obstructed by cold and wind, causing the blood to stagnate in the lower abdomen, it leads to painful blockages, referred to as child’s fontanelle pain.

    Lower Abdominal Pain

    Lower abdominal pain after childbirth is caused by the retention of lochia or an encounter with external cold. If it does not disperse over time, it will likely result in a blood mass, disrupting menstrual flow. If the cause is Qi stagnation, use Yan Hu Suo San. If due to external cold, use Wu Ji San. If caused by anger, use Si Wu San with Mu Xiang and Chai Hu. If due to blood deficiency, treat with Si Wu, Ren Shen, Bai Zhu, and Pao Jiang. If there is weakness in Yang Qi, use Si Jun Zi, Dang Gui, and Pao Jiang. If there is spleen deficiency and weak blood, treat with Liu Jun Zi, Dang Gui, and Pao Jiang.[32p]

    寒证腹痛

    　　产后脐腹作痛，乃冷气乘虚也，用当归建中汤治之。陈无择云：若产当寒月，人门、脐下胀痛，手不可近者，用羊肉汤治之。　　【注　前证若脾胃虚弱，寒邪所侵，用蟠葱散。若肝经湿热，小便不利，用龙胆泻肝汤。仍参前论，恐有瘀血为患。】
    　　　　　两胁胀痛

    　　产后两胁胀痛，因恶露不尽，或肝经血虚，或肝经气滞，当分而治之。　　【注　前证若肝经血瘀，用延胡索散。若肝经气滞，用四君、青皮、柴胡。若肝经血虚，用四物、参、朮、柴胡。气血俱虚，用八珍、柴胡。若肾水不足，不能生肝，用六味丸。若肺金势盛，克制肝木，用泻白散。仍参照前各论主之。】　　【按《大全》：产后两胁胀满气痛，由膀胱宿有停水，因产后恶露不下尽，水壅痞与气相搏，积在膀胱，故令胁肋胀满；气与水相激，故令痛也。】
    　　　　　积聚症块
    [33p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[33p]

    Cold-Related Abdominal Pain

    After childbirth, if there is pain around the navel and abdomen, it is due to cold air entering the body. This can be treated with Danggui Jianzhong Decoction. Chen Wuze states: If childbirth occurs in a cold month, with distension and pain below the navel where one cannot touch it, use lamb meat soup for treatment.

    Note: If the previous symptoms indicate a deficiency of the spleen and stomach with invasion of cold evil, use Pancang Herbal Powder. If there is damp heat in the liver channel with difficulty urinating, use Longdan Xiegan Decoction. Always consider the possibility of blood stasis as an issue.

    Side Flank Distension and Pain

    If there is distension and pain in the flanks after childbirth, it may be due to incomplete discharge of lochia, blood deficiency in the liver channel, or qi stagnation in the liver channel, and should be treated accordingly.

    Note: If blood stasis is present in the liver channel, use Yan Hu Suo Powder. If there is qi stagnation in the liver channel, use Si Junzi Decoction combined with Qingpi and Chaihu. If there is blood deficiency in the liver channel, use Si Wu Decoction with Ren Shen, Dioscorea, and Chaihu. If both qi and blood are deficient, use Ba Zhen Decoction with Chaihu. If there is insufficient kidney water and inability to nourish the liver, use Liu Wei Di Huang Wan. If there is excessive lung metal restraining the liver wood, use Xie Bai Powder. Always reference the previous discussions for guidance.

    Comment: According to the “Great Compendium,” distension and pain in the flanks after childbirth may result from stagnant water in the bladder due to incomplete discharge of lochia, leading to accumulation and blockage of qi in the bladder, causing flank distension and pain.[33p]

    夫积者阴气也，五脏所生；聚者阳气也，六腑所成。然积为阴，阴性沉伏，故痛不离其部；聚为阳，阳性浮动，故痛无常处。皆由饮食不节，起居失宜，产后血气虚弱，风冷所乘，搏于脏腑耳。　　【注　前证乃真气亏损，邪气乘之，况产后得之，尤当固元气为主。若求旦夕之效，而攻其邪则速其危矣。当参痃癖诸论治之。】　　【按《大全》：风冷所乘，搏于脏腑，与血气相结，故成积聚症块。】
    　　　　　血瘕

    　　产后瘀血，与气相搏，名曰瘕，谓其痛而无定处，此因夙有风冷而成，轻则痞塞，重则不通。　　【注　前证乃寒邪乘客，气血壅结，此因气病而血病也，当补养胃气，调和月经，宽缓静养为善。《难经》云：任脉之病，男子为七疝，女子为瘕聚。当参前后各论治之。】　　【按《大全》：瘕之言假也，谓其痛浮假无定处也。此由素有风冷血气不治，至产血下则少，故致此病也。不急治则多成积结，妨害月水，轻则痞塞，重则不通也。】[34p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[34p]

    Accumulation derives from yin qi, generated by the five organs; aggregation comes from yang qi, formed by the six bowels. Accumulation is yin, and its nature is to be hidden, hence the pain does not leave its site; aggregation is yang, with a nature that is floating, thus the pain is variable in location. Both arise from irregularities in diet, improper living habits, postnatal blood and qi deficiency, and the invasion of wind and cold, which confront the internal organs.

    The condition known as 'blood mass' occurs due to stagnant blood after childbirth, which clashes with qi; it is called 'mass' because the pain is indeterminate, arising from previous exposure to wind and cold. In mild cases, there is a feeling of distention; in severe cases, there is obstruction.

    The previous symptoms indicate that cold evil has invaded, leading to stagnation of qi and blood. This results in both qi and blood issues; one should nourish the stomach qi, regulate the menstrual cycle, and aim for gentle recuperation. The "Nanjing" states that disorders of the Ren meridian in men manifest as seven hernias, while in women they manifest as masses. One should refer to the discussions on treatment before and after.

    The term 'mass' implies that the pain is unstable and lacks a fixed location. This arises from previously existing conditions of wind, cold, and stagnant blood that go untreated, leading to reduced blood after childbirth, thus causing this ailment. If not treated urgently, it may lead to numerous accumulations, hindering menstrual flow. Light cases encounter distention, while severe cases face blockage.[34p]

    余血奔心烦闷

    　　产后余血奔心，若因分娩后不饮童便，以致虚火上炎所致，宜用金黄散或炒蒲黄三钱，水煎服。失笑散亦佳。　　【注　前证当与产后恶露上攻心痛同治。】　　【按《大全》：此证盖是分解了，不即饮童子小便，并擀心及卧太速，兼食不相宜之物所致。但能依方疗之，无不痊可。】
    　　　　　口干痞闷

    　　产后口干痞闷，因食米面干饭太早，脾胃不能消化。热郁所致，慎不可下。但服见晛丸则愈。无择云：若内积忧烦，外伤燥热，宜四物汤去地黄加人参、乌梅，可愈。　　【注　前证若宿食停滞，用六君、枳实、神曲；若因肉食所致，更加山楂；若因鱼鲙之类，再加陈皮。其物既消而仍痞，或反作痛作呕，此脾胃受伤，用六君子汤；或咽酸嗳腐加炮姜，作泻更加升麻；如不应，佐以四神丸，或间用补中益气汤。[35p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[35p]

    Remaining Blood Disturbing the Heart

    After childbirth, remaining blood can disturb the heart. If this happens due to not drinking the boy's urine after delivery, leading to excessive fire rising, it is advisable to use Jin Huang San or stir-fried Pu Huang, three qian, and decoct in water to take. Shi Xiao San is also good.
    (Note: This condition should be treated similarly to postnatal lochia attacking the heart causing pain.)
    (According to "Da Quan": This condition arises from unresolved issues, not immediately drinking boy's urine, and sleeping too quickly, combined with inappropriate foods. However, if one follows the prescriptions, healing is certain.)

    Dry Mouth and Distension

    After childbirth, if there is dry mouth and distension, it is due to eating dry rice too early, causing the spleen and stomach to be unable to digest and leading to heat stagnation. Be cautious not to induce anything further. However, taking Jian Xian Wan can lead to recovery.
    Wuzexuan states: If there is internal accumulation of worries and external injury from dryness and heat, it is advisable to use Si Wu Tang with the addition of Ren Shen and Wu Mei for recovery.
    (Note: If the previous condition is due to retained food stagnation, use Liu Jun Zi, Zhi Shi, and Shen Qu; if caused by meat, add more Shan Zha; if due to fish, add Chen Pi. If these items are digested yet still cause distension or pain or vomiting, this is a sign of spleen and stomach injury, use Liu Jun Zi Tang; or if there is sourness at the throat and rancid belching, add roasted ginger to induce a purge, additionally consider Sheng Ma; if these do not respond, assist with Si Shen Wan, or intermittently use Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang.)[35p]

    】　　【按郭氏曰：产后荣卫大伤，血气未定，食面太早，胃不能消化，面毒结聚于胃脘，上熏胸中，是以口干燥渴，心下痞闷，医者不识，认为胸膈壅滞，以药下之，万不得一。】　　【按无择云：产后口干痞闷，未必止因食面，或产母内积忧烦，外伤燥热，饮食甘肥，使口干痞闷，当随其所因调之可也。心烦宜四物汤去地黄加人参、乌梅煎。若外伤燥热，看属何经，当随经为治。难以备举。饮食所伤，见晛丸却能作效。】　　【按郭氏又云：或有产后劳伤虚羸，因事触忤，怒气上逆，以致胸膈痞塞，口干烦闷者，亦宜服见晛丸。盖其间药味，皆是顺气快膈之剂。紫苏饮亦可服。】[36p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[36p]

    According to Guo, after childbirth, the body's essence and blood are greatly depleted, and the vital energy is unsettled. Eating wheat flour too early can lead to indigestibility; the toxic substances accumulate in the stomach, rising to obstruct the chest. This causes dryness in the mouth, thirst, and a feeling of fullness and discomfort in the heart area. If doctors fail to recognize this, they may mistakenly believe it is due to obstruction in the chest and administer purgatives, which is utterly wrong.

    Wuzhe remarks that postnatal mouth dryness and chest discomfort may not solely stem from eating wheat flour. It could also be due to the mother's internal worries, external heat and dryness, or consumption of rich foods, which can lead to these symptoms. Treatment should be adjusted according to the underlying cause. For restlessness, a decoction of four herbs with the addition of ginseng and black plum can be used. If external heat is involved, treatment should be based on the affected meridian. It's challenging to list all possibilities. For dietary injuries, the Jianxian pill can be effective.

    Guo further states that there may be cases of postnatal fatigue and weakness, which, if affected by emotional disturbances, can lead to chest oppression and dryness. In such cases, the Jianxian pill should also be taken, as its ingredients are designed to soothe the qi and relieve chest tension. A decoction of perilla can also be consumed.[36p]

    寒热

    　　产后发热，头痛身疼，虽如伤寒时气，虽当用麻黄，亦不可轻易。如早起劳动，为寒所伤，则淅淅恶寒，翕翕发热，头项肩背骨节皆痛，至七八日乃瘥。若大便坚，作呕不能食，用小柴胡汤加生姜、地黄主之。　　【注　按《病机》云：治胎产之病，从厥阴经论之。无犯胃气及上中二焦，谓之三禁，不可汗，不可下，不可利小便。发汗者同伤寒下之早证，利大便则脉数而已动于脾，利小便则内亡津液，胃中枯燥。制药之法，能不犯三禁，则荣卫自和而寒热止矣。】　　产后乍寒乍热，由血气虚损，阴阳不和，若阴盛则乍寒，阳盛则乍热，宜用增损四物汤。若因败血不散，腹内作痛，宜用夺命丹，后用增损四物汤，随病加减。无择云：败血流闭诸阴则寒，流闭诸阳则热，用大调经散、五积散。[37p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[37p]

    Cold and Heat

    Postpartum fever, headache, and body aches can resemble the conditions associated with exogenous pathogenic factors. Although ephedra may be used, caution must be exercised. If one begins labor early in the day and is subsequently harmed by the cold, they will experience a chill followed by fever, with pain in the head, neck, shoulders, and joints, lasting seven to eight days before recovery. If there is hard stool, accompanied by nausea and inability to eat, one should use Xiao Chai Hu Decoction with added fresh ginger and rehmannia as the primary treatment.

    [Note: According to "Disease Mechanisms," the treatment of postpartum ailments is discussed in relation to the Jueyin meridian. One must avoid harming the stomach qi and the upper and middle jiao, known as the three prohibitions: sweating, purging, and promoting diuresis should be avoided. Sweating is associated with early symptoms of exogenous fever, while promoting bowel movement merely results in a rapid pulse and disturbance in the spleen, and promoting urination leads to the loss of body fluids and dryness in the stomach. The method of formulating medicine should not violate these three prohibitions; thus, when the nutritive and defensive energies are harmonized, cold and heat will cease.]

    Postpartum symptoms of alternating chills and fever arise from qi and blood deficiency, resulting in disharmony between yin and yang. If yin is excessive, one experiences chills; if yang is excessive, one experiences heat, and the use of Zengsuan Siwu Decoction is appropriate. If due to retained blood causing abdominal pain, Daoming Dan should be used, followed by Zengsuan Siwu Decoction, adjusting the formula according to the illness. According to Wu Ze, if retained blood obstructs the yin, it leads to cold; if it obstructs the yang, it leads to heat, and one should use Daodiao Jingsan or Wujisuan.[37p]

    【注　前证若因阳气不足，阴气上入于阳中而恶寒者，用补中益气汤。若因阴气不足，阳气下陷于阴中而发热者，用六味地黄丸。若血气不足而恶寒发热者，用八珍汤。若病后寒热倦怠者，用补中益气汤。若肌热大渴目赤面红者，用当归补血汤。按郭氏曰：阴胜则乍寒，阳胜则乍热，阴阳相乘则乍寒乍热。若因产劳伤脏腑，血气不得宣越，故令败血不散，入于肺则热，入于脾则寒。】　　阴阳不和，败血不散，何以别之？时有刺痛者败血也，但寒热无他证者，阴阳不和也。　　 【按郭氏又云：血属阴，气属阳。血气一伤，阴阳互相乘克，所以乍寒乍热。此特论阴阳不和之所由致者。亦有因产恶露下少，留滞胞络，亦令人寒热，但小腹痛急为异耳。阴阳不和宜服增损四物汤，败血停留宜服夺命丹或黑龙丹。增损四物汤，亦可兼进。】[38p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[38p]

    If the cause of cold is insufficient Yang energy, leading to Yin energy rising into Yang, then use "Buzhong Yiqi Decoction." If the cause of fever is insufficient Yin energy, leading to Yang energy sinking into Yin, then use "Liuwei Dihuang Wan." If there is insufficient blood energy causing both cold and fever, then use "Bazhen Tang." If there is fatigue and alternating cold and heat after illness, then use "Buzhong Yiqi Decoction." If there is muscle heat, great thirst, red eyes, and a flushed face, then use "Danggui Buxue Tang."

    It is noted that, according to Guo, when Yin prevails, there is sudden cold; when Yang prevails, there is sudden heat. When Yin and Yang interact, there are alternating cold and heat. If postpartum fatigue injures the organs, and blood energy cannot be expressed, it causes stagnant blood that becomes heat in the lungs and cold in the spleen.

    How can one differentiate when Yin and Yang are not harmonized and stagnant blood does not disperse? Sharp pain indicates stagnant blood, but if there are no other symptoms of cold or heat, it is due to the disharmony of Yin and Yang.

    Furthermore, Guo states that blood belongs to Yin and energy belongs to Yang. When blood and energy are both injured, Yin and Yang mutually counteract, resulting in alternating cold and heat. This specifically discusses the cause of disharmony between Yin and Yang. There may also be cases where postpartum lochia is insufficient and stagnates in the channels, causing cold and heat, but sharp pain in the lower abdomen indicates a different issue.

    For disharmony of Yin and Yang, it is advisable to take "Zengsun Siwu Decoction." For stagnant blood, one should take "Duoming Dan" or "Heilong Dan." "Zengsun Siwu Decoction" can be used in conjunction.[38p]

    疟疾

    　　郭稽中云：产后乍寒乍热者，多是败血为害，或阴阳不和，若概作疟疾治之，误矣。陈无择云：产后寒热，或一二日或二三日一发，或先寒后热，或先热后寒，或寒多热少，或热多寒少，或纯寒纯热者，皆是疟疾，最难治疗，可用草果饮、生熟饮、四兽饮选用之。　　【注　前证当与疟疾论参看用药，以补胃气为主，佐以草果饮之类。若胃气稍充，以草果饮为主，佐以补胃之剂。大抵产后疟疾。[39p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[39p]

    Malaria

    Guo Jizhong said: For those who experience alternating chills and fever after childbirth, it is often due to the harm of败血 (tainted blood) or an imbalance of yin and yang. If one indiscriminately treats this condition as malaria, it would be a mistake. Chen Wuze stated: After childbirth, if chills and fever occur, whether once every day or every two or three days, if the chills precede the fever or vice versa, or if chills are more pronounced than the fever, or if there is pure cold or pure heat — all of these are classified as malaria and are the most difficult to treat. One may use grass fruit decoction, raw and cooked decoction, or Four Beasts decoction as options. [Note: For the above symptoms, one should refer to the treatment of malaria when selecting medicines, primarily focusing on replenishing stomach qi, supplemented with grass fruit decoction. If stomach qi is somewhat sufficient, then primarily use grass fruit decoction, combined with tonics for the stomach. Generally, this pertains to postpartum malaria.][39p]

    因脾胃虚弱，饮食停滞，或外邪所感，或郁怒伤脾，或暑邪所伏。审系饮食，用六君加桔梗、苍朮、藿香。如外邪多而饮食少，用藿香正气散。如外邪少而饮食多，用人参养胃汤。劳役所伤，用补中益气汤。气血虚弱，用十全大补加炮姜。中气虚寒，用六君加姜、桂，元气脱陷急加附子。盖气虚则寒，血虚则热，胃虚则恶寒，胃气下陷则寒热交作，或吐泻不食，腹痛烦渴，发热讝语，或手足逆冷，寒战如栗，虽见百证，但温补脾胃，其病自退。若误用清脾饮，则中气伤而变证多矣。大凡产后久疟，多属元气虚寒。】[40p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[40p]

    Due to weakness of the spleen and stomach, there may be food stagnation, or it could be caused by external pathogens, or emotional disturbances damaging the spleen, or heat invasion. Carefully regulate the diet, using Six Gentlemen Decoction with added Platycodon, Atractylodes, and Agastache. If there are many external pathogens and little to eat, use Agastache Qi Regulation Powder. If there are few external pathogens but much food intake, use Ginseng Nourishing the Stomach Decoction. If injured by labor, use Tonifying the Middle and Benefiting Qi Decoction. For weakness of qi and blood, use All-Encompassing Great Tonic with roasted ginger added. For cold due to deficiency of middle qi, use Six Gentlemen Decoction with added ginger and cinnamon; if the original qi has sunken and is urgent, add Aconite. For when qi is deficient, it leads to cold; for blood deficiency, it leads to heat; for stomach deficiency, it causes aversion to cold; if stomach qi has sunk, it leads to alternating cold and heat, or vomiting, diarrhea, inability to eat, abdominal pain, thirst, fever, confusion, or cold extremities with shivering like a cold chill. Despite presenting various symptoms, if warmth and tonification of the spleen and stomach are applied, the disease will naturally retreat. Using Clear the Spleen Decoction mistakenly, however, will damage the middle qi and lead to multiple disorders. Generally, prolonged malaria after childbirth often relates to deficiency of original qi and cold.[40p]

    蓐劳

    　　夫产后蓐劳者，此由生产日浅，血气虚弱，饮食未平复，不满日月，气血虚羸，将养失所，而风冷客之。风冷搏于气血，则不能温于肌肤，使人虚乏劳倦，乍卧乍起，容颜憔悴，食饮不消。风冷邪气而感于肺，肺受微寒，故咳嗽口干，遂觉头昏百节疼痛，荣卫受于风邪，流注脏腑，须臾频发，时有盗汗，寒热如疟，背髆烦闷，四肢不举，沉重着床，此则蓐劳之候也。　　妇人因产里不顺，疲极筋力，忧劳心虑，致令虚羸喘乏，寒热如疟，头痛自汗，肢体倦怠，咳嗽痰逆，腹中绞刺，名曰蓐劳。[41p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[41p]

    Ruluo (Postpartum Fatigue)

    After giving birth, the condition known as ruluo occurs due to the recent nature of childbirth, wherein the blood and qi are weak, and eating and drinking have not yet stabilized. The body has not fully recovered, and with the deficiency of qi and blood, there is a failure of nourishment, allowing wind and cold to invade.

    When the wind and cold disrupt the qi and blood, they cannot warm the skin, resulting in a feeling of fatigue and weakness, with shifts between lying down and getting up, a haggard appearance, and digestive issues. The invasion of wind and cold affects the lungs, causing mild cold symptoms, leading to cough and dry mouth, accompanied by dizziness and pain throughout the joints, as well as fluctuations in bodily fluids.

    This condition may result in spontaneous sweating, intermittent chills and fever resembling malaria, discomfort in the back and sides, weakness in the limbs, and a heavy sensation that keeps one in bed—these are symptoms of ruluo.

    In women, if delivery is not smooth, extreme fatigue can occur, accompanied by worry and anxiety, which lead to the state of weakness and breathlessness, fluctuating chills and fever, headaches with spontaneous sweating, fatigue in the limbs, coughing with phlegm, and abdominal pain—a condition referred to as ruluo.[41p]

    【注　前证当扶养正气为主，用六君子汤加当归。若脾肺气虚而咳嗽口干，用补中益气加麦冬、五味子。若因中气虚而口干头运，用补中益气加蔓荆子。若肝经血虚而肢体作痛，用四物、参、朮。若因肝肾虚弱而自汗盗汗，寒热往来者，用六味丸加五味子。若因脾虚血弱，肚腹作痛，月经不调，用八珍汤倍加白朮。若因脾虚血燥，皮肤瘙痒，用加味逍遥散。大抵此证多因脾胃虚弱，饮食减少，以致诸经疲惫而作，当补脾胃，饮食一进，精气生化，诸脏有所倚赖，其病自愈矣。仍照虚烦发热方论主治。】[42p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[42p]

    This text provides guidelines for treating various conditions based on different types of Qi and blood deficiencies.

    1. For main support of righteous Qi, use Liu Jun Zi Tang (Six Gentleman Decoction) with added Dang Gui (Angelica sinensis).

    2. If there is deficiency in the spleen and lung causing cough and dry mouth, use Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang (Tonify the Middle and Benefit Qi Decoction) with Mai Dong (Ophiopogon) and Wu Wei Zi (Schisandra).

    3. If dry mouth and head heaviness arise from central Qi deficiency, use Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang with Man Jing Zi (Vitex).

    4. If there is liver meridian blood deficiency causing body aches, use Si Wu Tang (Four Substance Decoction) with Ren Shen (Ginseng) and Bai Zhu (Atractylodes).

    5. For spontaneous sweating due to liver and kidney weakness, with alternating chills and fever, use Liu Wei Di Huang Wan (Six-Flavor Rehmannia Pill) with Wu Wei Zi.

    6. If abdominal pain and irregular menstruation stem from spleen deficiency and blood weakness, use Ba Zhen Tang (Eight Treasure Decoction) with an extra measure of Bai Zhu.

    7. For skin itching due to spleen deficiency and dry blood, use Jia Wei Xiao Yao San (Modified Rambling Powder).

    In general, these conditions often arise from weakness in the spleen and stomach, leading to reduced appetite and exhaustion of the channels. Thus, strengthening the spleen and stomach is essential. Once the appetite improves and vital energy is generated, the organs will be supported, and the illness can resolve itself. Further reference should be made to methods for treating deficient irritability and fever.[42p]

    虚羸

    　　《产宝》云：产后虚羸者，皆因产后亏损血气所致，当慎起居，节饮食六淫七情，调养百日，庶保无疾。若中年及难产者，无论日期，必须调养平复，方可治事，否则气血复伤，虚羸之证作矣。　　【注　前证产伤气血者，用八珍汤。饮食伤胃者，用四君子汤。停食伤脾者，用六君子汤。劳伤元气者，用补中益气汤。若嗳气觉有药味者，此药复伤胃也，但用四君子汤徐徐少饮，以调脾胃。若胃气一健，血气自生，诸证自愈矣。】[43p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[43p]

    Xu Li

    The "Chan Bao" states: Those who are weak after childbirth have suffered a loss of blood and vital energy. It is essential to be cautious in one's daily routine and moderate in diet, avoiding excessive emotions and desires, and nurturing oneself for a hundred days, to ensure the absence of illness. For those of middle age or those who had difficult deliveries, regardless of the time elapsed, it is necessary to stabilize and nurture oneself before attending to other matters; otherwise, the vital energy and blood may be further harmed, leading to symptoms of weakness.

    [Notes: For symptoms resulting from childbirth that injure energy and blood, use the Eight Treasure Decoction. For dietary injuries to the stomach, use the Four Gentlemen Decoction. For food stagnation harming the spleen, use the Six Gentlemen Decoction. For exertion damaging the root energy, use the Qi Invigorating Decoction. If there is a feeling of gas with a medicinal taste, this medicine may further harm the stomach; instead, drink the Four Gentlemen Decoction slowly and in small amounts to regulate the spleen and stomach. Once the stomach qi is strong, the blood and vital energy will naturally replenish, and all symptoms will resolve.][43p]

    风虚冷劳

    　　产后气血劳伤，脏腑虚弱，若风冷乘之，血气愈虚，形气羸损，风冷入脏，令人无子。谓之风虚冷劳也。　　【注　前证若血气虚弱，用八珍汤。血气虚寒，用十全大补汤。胃气虚弱，用补中益气汤。脾气虚弱，用六君子汤。命门火衰，用八味丸。肝脾血虚，用加味逍遥散。肝脾郁怒，用加味归脾汤。】　　【按《大全》：风冷搏于血气，血气不能温于肌肤，使人虚乏疲顿，致羸损不平复。若久不平复，风冷入于子脏，则胞脏冷，使人无子。】[44p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[44p]

    Wind and Cold Exhaustion

    Postpartum, the energy and blood are depleted, and the organs are weakened. If wind and cold invade, the blood and energy become even more deficient, leading to a frail body. When wind and cold enter the organs, it results in infertility. This is called wind and cold exhaustion.

    [Note: If the symptoms indicate blood and energy deficiency, use the Ba Zhen Tang. For blood and energy deficiency with cold, use the Shi Quan Da Bu Tang. For weak stomach energy, use the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang. For weak spleen energy, use the Liu Jun Zi Tang. If the life gate fire is weak, use the Ba Wei Wan. For liver and spleen blood deficiency, use the Jia Wei Xiao Yao San. For liver and spleen stagnation due to anger, use the Jia Wei Gui Pi Tang.]

    [According to "The Complete Compendium": Wind and cold impacting the blood and energy prevent them from warming the skin, leading to fatigue and a frail state that cannot recover. If it does not recover for a long time, wind and cold can enter the reproductive organs, causing them to become cold and resulting in infertility.][44p]

    腹胀呕吐

    　　产后腹胀满闷呕吐者，因败血散于脾胃，不能运化而致，宜用抵圣汤治之。　　【注　前证若败血散于脾胃，宜用前方。若饮食停于脾，宜用六君、厚朴。若饮食伤于胃，宜用六君子汤。大凡损其脾者，当节其饮食为善。】　　【按郭氏曰：败血散于脾胃，脾受之则不能运化精微而成腹胀，胃受之则不能受纳水谷而生吐逆。医者不识，若以寻常治胀止吐药治之，病与药不相干，转更伤动正气，疾愈难治，但服抵圣汤则愈。】[45p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[45p]

    Abdominal Distension and Vomiting

    For postpartum abdominal distension, fullness, and vomiting, it is caused by the dispersal of putrid blood throughout the spleen and stomach, which prevents digestion and transformation. It is advisable to use the Dispersing Holy Decoction for treatment.

    Note: If putrid blood disperses in the spleen and stomach, the aforementioned prescription should be used. If food stagnates in the spleen, one should use Six Gentlemen Decoction and Hou Po. If food injures the stomach, the Six Gentlemen Decoction should be employed. Generally, if the spleen is damaged, it is wise to regulate one's diet.

    Commentary: Guo states: When putrid blood disperses in the spleen and stomach, if the spleen is affected, it cannot transport vital essence, leading to abdominal distension. If the stomach is affected, it cannot receive and assimilate food and water, resulting in vomiting. If physicians do not recognize this, and mistakenly treat the distension and vomiting with common remedies, the illness will not be related to the medicine, and it will further harm the proper Qi, making recovery difficult. However, by simply taking the Dispersing Holy Decoction, one can recover.[45p]

    呕逆不食

    　　经云：胃为水谷之海以养脏腑，因产后胃气虚弱，饮食所伤，必致呕逆，故不食也。　　【注　前证若因饮食过时，用四君子汤。饮食过多，用六君子汤。饮食过时而兼劳役，用补中益气。若因饮食停滞，用人参养胃汤。脾胃气虚，用六君子汤。胃气虚寒，加炮姜、木香。寒水侮土，用益黄散。肝木侮脾土，用六君、升麻、柴胡。命门火衰不能生土，用八味丸。呕吐泄泻，手足俱冷，或肚腹作痛，乃阳气虚寒，急用附子理中汤，多有生者。】　　【按《大全》：胃输水谷之精，以为血气，荣润脏腑，因产则脏腑伤动，有时而气独盛者，则气乘肠胃，肠胃燥濇，其气则逆，故呕吐不下食也。】[46p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[46p]

    Nausea and refusal to eat

    The classics say: The stomach is the sea of food and water that nourishes the organs. Therefore, after childbirth, when stomach qi is weakened and food injures it, vomiting occurs, leading to a refusal to eat.

    [Note: If the symptoms are due to food being consumed too late, use the Four Gentlemen Decoction. If food intake is excessive, use the Six Gentlemen Decoction. If food is consumed late along with overexertion, use the Ren Shen Yang Wei Decoction. If due to food stagnation, use the Ren Shen Yang Wei Decoction. For spleen and stomach qi deficiency, use the Six Gentlemen Decoction. If there is cold in the stomach with weak qi, add roasted ginger and costus. If cold water invades the earth, use Yi Huang San. If liver wood overacts on spleen earth, use Six Gentlemen, Sheng Ma, and Chai Hu. If the kidney fire is weak and cannot generate earth, use the Eight Treasure Pill. For vomiting and diarrhea with cold hands and feet or abdominal pain, it indicates a deficiency of yang qi and cold; urgently use Fu Zi Li Zhong Decoction, as many recover from this condition.]

    [According to the "Complete Compendium": The stomach transports the essence of food and water, creating blood and qi, nourishing the organs. After childbirth, the organs may be damaged, sometimes leading to excess qi that causes it to rebel in the intestines and stomach, resulting in dryness and consequently, nausea and inability to retain food.][46p]

    霍乱

    　　产后霍乱，因脏腑虚损，饮食不消，触冒风冷所致。若热而饮水者，五苓散。寒而不饮水者，理中丸；虚冷者，加附子。来复丹尤妙。　　【注　前证当与后产后腹痛泻利方论，互相主治。】　　【按《大全》：阴阳不顺，清浊相干，气乱于肠胃之间，真邪相搏，冷热不调，上吐下利，故曰霍乱也。】
    　　　　　头痛

    　　夫头者诸阳之会也。产后胃气虚弱，饮食少思，阳气微弱，不能上升，故头痛。若因败血，黑龙丹言之甚详。　　【注　前证若中气虚，用补中益气汤加蔓荆子。若血虚，用四物加参、朮。血气俱虚，用八珍汤。若因风寒所伤，用补中益气汤加川芎。】　　【按《大全》：产后五脏皆虚，胃气亏弱，饮食不充，谷气尚乏，则令虚热；阳气不守，上凌于头，阳实阴虚，则令头痛也。又有产后败血头痛，不可不知。】[47p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[47p]

    Cholera
