    For postpartum blood stasis: take 20 peach kernels, remove skin and tips, and one piece of lotus root, boil with water and consume; it is effective.

    For postpartum diarrhea with red and white discharge and a heavy sensation after urgency: take dried peach gum, and equal parts of sandalwood and cattail pollen, grind into powder, and take 2 qian before meals with rice drink.

    For postpartum blood stagnation causing disturbance in the heart: take 5 liang of ginger, boil in 8 sheng of water until reduced to 3 sheng, and divide into three doses.[273p]

    产后垂出肉线，长三四尺，触之痛引心腹欲绝：老姜连皮三斤，捣烂，入麻油二斤，拌匀炒干，先以熟绢五尺，折作方结，令人轻轻盛起肉线，使之屈曲作三团，纳入产户；乃以绢袋盛姜，就近熏之。冷则更换。经一日夜，缩入大半，二日尽入。但不可使线断，断则不可治矣。
    　　产后下痢赤白者：用紫苋菜一握，切煮汁，入粳米三合，煮粥食之，瘥。 【《本草》】
    　　产后虚汗：马齿苋研汁三合服。如无，以干者煮汁。
    　　产后痢渴久病，津液枯竭，四肢浮肿，口舌干燥：用冬瓜一枚，外以黄土泥厚五寸，煨熟绞汁饮。亦治伤寒痢渴。【《录验》】
    　　产后血疼欲死者：槐耳半两为末，酒浓煎饮之，立愈。 【《妇人良方》，下同】
    　　产后败血邪气入心，如见祟物，颠狂：用大辰砂一、二钱研细飞过，用饮儿乳汁三四茶匙调湿，以紫项地龙一条，入药滚三滚，刮净，去地龙不用，入无灰酒一盏，分作三四次服。【何氏方】[274p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[274p]

    After childbirth, if there is a protruding flesh line, measuring three to four feet, touching it causes pain that radiates to the heart and abdomen, take three pounds of old ginger with the skin, pound it into a paste, mix with two pounds of sesame oil, stir-fry until dry. First, take five feet of cooked silk, fold it into a square knot, and gently lift the flesh line, allowing it to bend into three loops and insert into the birth canal. Then, put the ginger in a cloth bag and smoke it nearby. Change the bag when it cools. After one day and night, it will retract more than half, and by the second day, it will be entirely retracted. However, it must not be broken; if it breaks, it cannot be treated.

    For postpartum diarrhea, whether red or white: use a handful of purple amaranth, chop and boil to extract the juice, then add three measures of glutinous rice, cook it into porridge and eat it to recover.

    For postpartum excessive sweating: grind three measures of purslane into juice and take that. If unavailable, boil the dried herb to extract juice.

    For prolonged diarrhea and thirst after childbirth, with dry fluids, swollen limbs, and dry mouth and tongue: take one winter melon, cover it with five inches of yellow clay, bake it until tender, and extract the juice to drink. This also treats typhus, diarrhea, and thirst.

    For severe abdominal pain after childbirth, feeling like dying: take half a tael of sophora japonica (other grain) and grind into powder, then brew in strong liquor and drink it for immediate relief.

    For postpartum evil blood and Qi invading the heart, leading to delirium: take one or two qian of realgar, grind it finely to dust, mix with three to four teaspoons of nursing milk for moisture. Use one purple earthworm, boil it in the medicine three times, scrape clean, discard the worm, and mix with one cup of ash-free liquor, dividing into three or four doses to take.[274p]

    妇人断产：白玉簪花、白凤仙子各一钱半，紫葳二钱半，辰砂一钱，捣末，蜜和丸梧子大，产内三十丸，日以酒半盏服之。不可着牙齿，能损牙齿也。【《摘元方》，下同】
    　　产后呕水：因怒哭伤肝，呕青绿水，用韭叶二斤取汁，入姜汁少许和饮即愈。
    　　治腹中及产后瘀血：红曲浸酒煮服。 【《本草》】
    　　治产后百病，或血热觉有余血水气，或中风困笃，或背强口噤，或但烦热瘈瘲，五缓六急口渴，或身头皆肿，或身痒呕逆直视，或手足顽痹，头旋眼眩，此皆虚热中风也：用大豆三升熬熟至微烟出，入瓶中，以酒五升沃口，经一日以上，服酒一升，温覆，令少汗出，身润即愈。口噤者，加独活半斤，微微捶破同沃之。产后宜常服以防风气。又消结血。盖此酒治污血，又能发表也。　　又方：以豆五升，酒一非焠之。[275p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[275p]

    For stopping postpartum bleeding: Use one and a half coins each of white jade hairpin flowers and white balsam, two and a half coins of purple wisteria, one coin of cinnabar, pound into a fine powder, mix with honey, and form into thirty pills the size of a walnut. Take three pills daily with half a cup of wine. Avoid contact with teeth, as it can damage them.

    For postpartum vomiting (due to anger or crying affecting the liver): If experiencing greenish liquid vomiting, take two pounds of chives to extract juice, mix with a little ginger juice, and drink to recover.

    For treating abdominal issues and postpartum blood stasis: Consume red yeast soaked in wine and boiled.

    For various postpartum ailments: This may encompass excessive heat from blood leading to excess blood and vapor, wind causing heaviness, back pain with mouth tightness, feelings of heat and restlessness, thirst from mild swelling, body itchiness and nausea, or leg and hand paralysis, dizziness, and blurred vision—all of which signify wind and heat deficiency: use three sheng of soybeans cooked until faint smoke arises, place in a bottle, and pour in five sheng of wine, steep for over a day. Take one sheng of this wine warm, allowing for slight sweating, which will relieve the symptoms. For those with tight mouths, add half a jin of common angelica, gently pound it, and mix it in the infusion. It is advisable to take regularly to prevent wind issues and to resolve blood clots. This wine treats stale blood and aids in expelling wind.

    Another formula: Use five sheng of soybeans and one sheng of wine to create an infusion.[275p]

    产后中寒，遍身冷直，□噤不识人：白朮一两，泽泻一两，生姜五钱，水一升，煎服。
    　　产后呕逆，别无他疾者：白朮一两二钱，生姜一两五钱，酒水各二升，煎一升，分三服。【《本草》】
    　　产后烦躁：禹余粮一枚，状如酸枣者，入地埋一半，紧筑，用炭一斤煅之，湿土罨一宿，打破去外面石，取里面细者研，水淘五七度，日干，再研万遍，用甘草汤服二钱，一服立效。【《经验方》】
    　　产后舌出不收：丹砂敷之，暗掷盆碎堕地作声惊之，自收。 【《集简方》】
    　　产后中风，身如角弓反张，口噤不谙：川乌头五两，銼块，黑大豆半升同炒半黑，以酒三升倾锅内急搅，以绢滤取酒，微温服一小盏取汗。若口不开，拗开灌之。未效，加乌鸡粪一合炒，纳酒中服，以瘥为度。【《小品方》，下同】[276p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[276p]

    Postpartum cold, body feels cold all over, unable to recognize others: Use one tael of Atractylodes, one tael of Alisma, five qian of fresh ginger, and one liter of water, decoct and take.

    Postpartum vomiting without other ailments: Use one tael and two qian of Atractylodes, one tael and five qian of fresh ginger, two liters each of wine and water, decoct to one liter, and divide into three doses.

    Postpartum unease: One piece of Yuyuliang, resembling a sour date, bury half in the ground tightly packed, use one jin of charcoal to calcine, cover with moist soil for one night, break away the outer stones, take the fine inner part, grind, wash five to seven times with water, air dry, then grind ten thousand times, take two qian with Licorice decoction, immediate effect.

    Postpartum tongue out and not retracting: Apply cinnabar, secretly drop a broken pot to create a sound and startle it back in.

    Postpartum stroke, body bent like a bow, mouth closed and unable to speak: Use five liang of Aconite, chopped, half a sheng of black soybeans, fry until half black, pour three liters of wine into the pan and stir urgently, filter the wine with silk, take warm one small cup to induce sweating. If the mouth does not open, pry it open and administer. If ineffective, add one handful of black chicken dung, fry, then take with wine for recovery.[276p]

    产后阴肿痛，用桃仁细研涂之。
    　　又方：五倍子、枯白矾为末，研桃仁为膏涂之。
    　　产后阴脱：人屎炒赤为末，酒服方寸匕，日二服。 【《千金》】
    　　妇人断产：零陵香为末，酒服二钱，每服至一两，即一年绝孕，盖血闻香即散也。 【《集要》】
    　　产后阴翻，产后燥热，遂成翻花：泽兰四两，煎汤熏洗二三次，再入枯矾煎洗之即安。【《集简方》】
    　　妇人断产：蚕子故纸方圆一尺烧为末，酒服，终身不产。 【《千金》】　　又方：剪印纸有印处烧灰，水服一钱匕，效。 【藏器】　　又方：用白面曲一升，无灰酒五升，打作糊，煮二升半，用绢帛滤去滓，作三服，候月经将来前日晚下吃一服，次日五更吃一服，天明吃一服，且经即行，终身绝子。　　又方：用油煎水银一日方息，空心服枣核大一丸，永断孕，不损人。[277p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[277p]

    For postpartum swelling and pain, grind peach kernels finely and apply them.

    Another recipe: mix fivefold sumac and burnt white alum into a powder, then grind peach kernels into a paste and apply.

    For uterine prolapse after childbirth: fry human feces into a powder, take a pinch with wine twice daily.

    For women who have stopped menstruating: powder zeroing fragrance and take two qian with wine each time until it totals one liang to prevent pregnancy for a year, as blood disperses upon smelling the fragrance.

    For postpartum inversion and dryness leading to blooming inversion: use four liang of common comfrey, boil in water for two to three washes, then add burnt alum for washing to stabilize.

    For women who have stopped menstruating: burn a foot-long piece of cicada molting paper to a powder, take with wine to prevent future pregnancies for life.

    Another recipe: burn printed paper from a stamp, take its ash with water for one qian, it is effective.

    Another recipe: mix one sheng of white flour yeast with five sheng of non-ash wine, make a dough, boil down to two and a half sheng, filter through fine silk, take in three doses before the evening before menstruation comes, again at the fifth watch, and once at dawn; thereafter, regular menstruation will resume, preventing future conception.

    Another recipe: fry mercury in oil for a day, then take a ball the size of a date stone on an empty stomach to permanently prevent pregnancy without harming the body.[277p]

    产后遗尿或尿数：桑螵蛸炙半两，龙骨一两为末，每米饮服二钱。 【《胎产方》】
    　　妇人胎前产后遗尿：白薇、芍药各一两为末，酒服方寸匕，日三服。 【《千金》】
    　　产后腹痛：用五露脂末，神曲糊丸，白朮陈皮汤下。 【丹溪方】
    　　儿枕作痛：五灵脂慢炒研末，酒服二钱。 【《产宝方》】
    　　　　针灸

    　　《甲乙经》曰：妇人产后余疾，食饮不下，胸胁搘满，目眩足寒，心切痛，善噫，闻酸臭，胀痹腹满，少腹尤大，期门主之。　　《千金方》曰：堕胎后，漏下五色，疼痛，灸胞门，穴在关门左边二寸是也。右边二寸，名子户。　　断产，灸右踝上一寸二壮即断。[278p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[278p]

    Postpartum urinary incontinence or frequency: Half a tael of roasted mulberry silkworm and one tael of dragon bone, ground into powder, taken with two qian in water.

    For women experiencing urinary issues before and after childbirth: One tael each of white vitex and peony, ground into powder, taken with a small spoonful of wine, three times a day.

    For postpartum abdominal pain: Use Wuluzhi powder, form into pills with shenqu, and take with a white atractylodes and dried tangerine peel decoction.

    For pain in the child's occipital region: Slow-fried Wulingzhi, ground into powder, taken with two qian of wine.

    Acupuncture

    The "Jia Yi Jing" states: For postpartum ailments in women, if there is inability to eat or drink, fullness in the chest and sides, dizziness, cold feet, sharp heart pain, reflux, and a sour smell, along with abdominal distension and fullness, especially in the lower abdomen, the Qimen point should be addressed. The "Qian Jin Fang" states: After miscarriage, if there is a discharge of five colors and pain, moxibustion at the Baomen point is recommended, located two inches to the left of the Guanmmen point. Two inches to the right is called the Zihu point. To stop postpartum bleeding, apply moxibustion one inch and two zhang above the right ankle.[278p]

    朱震亨《心法》曰：妇人四旬，因小产成病，百节痛无常处，卧床不起，刺八髎五分，环跳四寸半，五枢三寸半，曲池、液门各寸半，绝骨二寸半。如脊背痛者，刺人中、大椎节下各五分，委中七分见血，立效。　　妇人五旬经断后再行，或多或少，或瘀或红并下，腹中气满如胎孕，刺天枢、中脘、气海三穴各五分。立愈。妇人得子，多变成水，淋漓而下，经久身面虚肿，刺阴谷二寸半，绝骨二寸半。如喘满，刺鱼际透大渊，左右共四十九呼，治肺经水气极妙。　　产后血块痛，三阴交、气海，宜灸之。　　妇人产后，忽小腹胀如蛊，大小便不通，气海、三里、关元、三阴交、阴谷各主之。　　《医学纲目》曰：产后手足逆冷，刺肩井立愈。　　产后血运不省人事，取三里、支沟、三阴交、心术 【无此一穴。】　　又法：取神门、内关；不应，取关元灸之。　　胞衣不下，取三阴文、中极，各泻之。　　产后噫呃服药无效，灸期门必愈。[279p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[279p]

    Zhu Zhenheng's "Heart Method" states: For women in the fourth month, if they suffer a miscarriage and develop an illness, experiencing pain throughout their body without a consistent location, unable to rise from bed, acupuncture should be applied to the eight sacral points at five minutes, the hip (Huan Tiao) four and a half inches from the center, and the five joints (Wu Shu) three and a half inches. For Qu Chi and Ye Men, each should be one and a half inches, and for Jue Gu, two and a half inches. If there is pain in the back, needle the Ren Zhong and Da Zhui points, five minutes each, and Wèi Zhōng to draw blood at seven minutes, leading to immediate effectiveness.

    When a woman returns to her cycles after five months, whether the flow is more or less, red or dark, accompanied by fullness in the abdomen as if pregnant, acupuncture should be done at Tian Shu, Zhong Wan, and Qi Hai, each at five minutes, leading to immediate recovery. If a woman becomes pregnant, often losing large amounts of water, dripping down for an extended period causing her body and face to swell, needle Yin Gu at two and a half inches and Jue Gu at two and a half inches. If there is dyspnea, needle the space between the fish and the Great Abyss, with a total of forty-nine breaths on both sides to treat excess water in the lung meridian.

    For blood clots and pain after childbirth, acupuncture at San Yin Jiao and Qi Hai is advisable. If after childbirth the lower abdomen swells as if burdened, and there is difficulty with urination and defecation, acupuncture should focus on Qi Hai, San Li, Guan Yuan, San Yin Jiao, and Yin Gu.

    The "Medical Compendium" states: If after childbirth the hands and feet become cold, needle Jian Jing for immediate recovery. If the blood circulation stops and the person is unconscious, target San Li, Zhi Gou, San Yin Jiao, and the heart point (note: this point is not listed).

    Another method includes using Shen Men and Nei Guan; if ineffective, target Guan Yuan with moxibustion. If the placenta is retained, focus on San Yin Wen and Zhong Ji for draining. If a woman experiences belching or hiccups after childbirth and medication proves ineffective, moxibustion at Qi Men will surely bring about recovery.[279p]

    《医学入门》曰：神门，治产后腹胀，小便不通。　　《医学准绳六要》曰：产后呃逆，急灸期门，乳头向下尽处是穴。乳小者，以一指为率。　　产后咳逆，灸期门极效，男 九 女右。乳下黑尽处一韭叶，灸三壮，甚者二七壮。　　《证治准绳》曰：产后小便不通，腹胀如鼓，闷乱不醒，缘未产之前，内积冷气，遂致产时尿胞运动不顺，用盐于产脐中填可与脐平，却用葱白剥去粗皮十余根作一缚，切作一指厚，安盐上，用大艾炷满葱饼子灸之，觉热气直入腹内，即时便通，神验不可具述。　　阴脱产门不闭，灸脐下横纹五七壮。　　产后血运不省人事，灸阳别。[280p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[280p]

    "The Introduction to Medicine" states: The Shenmen point treats postpartum abdominal distension and difficulty urinating. "The Six Essentials of Medical Guidelines" says: For postpartum hiccups, apply moxibustion at the Qimen point, located just below the nipple. If the breast is small, use one finger as a measurement. For postpartum coughing and hiccups, moxibustion at the Qimen point is highly effective, with males using nine strokes and females using right. Below the breast at the darkest point, place a chive leaf, applying three strokes of moxibustion, or in severe cases, two sets of seven strokes. "The Principles of Diagnosis and Treatment" states: For postpartum difficulty urinating and abdominal distension like a drum, with confusion and inability to wake, this is due to the internal accumulation of cold air before giving birth, resulting in uncoordinated bladder movement during labor. Fill the navel with salt to be level with it; then use about ten strips of scallion, peeled of their coarse skin, bound together, cut to the thickness of one finger, place the salt on top, and apply moxa fully over the scallion bundle until a sensation of heat penetrates the abdomen; this will facilitate urination immediately, and the effects are extraordinarily effective, beyond words. For uterine prolapse with the birth canal not closing, apply moxibustion five to seven strokes below the navel where the horizontal line is. If blood circulation is impaired postpartum and the person is unresponsive, apply moxibustion at Yangbai point."[280p]

    医案

    　　《疮疡全书》曰：一少妇产儿后，忽玉户垂尺许，状如肠，少不知事，私以手摘断，至晚，腹痛号泣而绝，此肠即生肠，又曰胞户子宫，切不可损，损则伤生。盖气血衰败，未能收入，宜多进活血之剂，三二日间，自然收人，不足忧恐也。予治三四人，皆以此药得力。
    　　朱震亨《心法》曰：一妇人产子后，阴户中下一物，如合钵状，有二岐，其夫来求治。予思之，此子宫也，必气血弱而下坠，遂用升麻、当归、黄芪大料二贴与之。半日后，其夫复来，日服二次后，觉响一声，视之，已收人阴户。但因经宿干着席上，破一片如掌心大，落在席，其妻在家哭泣，恐肠破不可复生。予思之，此非肠胃，乃糟粕也。肌肉破尚可复完，若气血充盛，必可生满，遂用四物汤加人参与一百贴，三年后有子。[281p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[281p]

    Medical Case

    The "Complete Book of Sores and Ulcers" states: A young woman, after giving birth, suddenly had her vagina protruding about a foot, resembling intestines. Being unaware of the situation, she privately pulled it down and by evening, experienced abdominal pain, crying out until she lost consciousness. This protrusion was indeed the intestines, also referred to as the uterus; it must not be damaged, for that would harm life. It was likely due to her qi and blood being weak, unable to retract properly. She should be given more medications to invigorate the blood. Within two or three days, it would naturally retract, and there would be no cause for concern. I have treated three or four people with this medicine and they all found relief.

    Zhu Zhenheng in "The Heart Method" states: A woman, after childbirth, had something like a bowl-shaped object descending from her vagina with two points. Her husband came seeking treatment. I considered this to be the uterus, likely due to weak qi and blood causing it to sag. Hence, I prescribed two applications of Angelica sinensis, Cang Zhu, and Astragalus. Half a day later, her husband returned; after taking the medicine twice daily, a sound was heard, and it was found to have retracted into the vagina. However, due to lying on the bedding overnight, a piece about the size of a palm had torn off and fallen onto the bedding, while his wife cried at home, fearful that her intestines had ruptured and could not regrow. I pondered this; it was not intestines, but rather remnants. If muscle is torn, it can still heal; with abundant qi and blood, it can fully regenerate. Thus, I used the Four Substances Decoction, adding powdered human placenta, and after one hundred doses, three years later she bore a child.[281p]

    一妇人面白，素多欲，产后血运，不知人事，急于气海灼艾五十五壮，遂苏；连进人参、黄芪、当归等补药，二月安。
    　　一妇人三十余岁，生女二日后，产户一物如手帕，下有帕尖，约重一斤。予思之，此因胎前劳乏伤气成肝痿所致，却喜血不甚虚，其时岁暮天寒，恐冷干坏了，急与炙黄芪半钱，人参一钱，白朮五分，当归一钱半，升麻五分，三贴连服之，即收上，得汗通身乃安。但下面沾席处，干者落一片，约五六两重，盖脂膜也。食进得眠，诊其脉皆濇左略弦，视其形却实，与白朮、芍药各钱半，陈皮一钱，生姜一片，煎二三贴以养之。[282p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[282p]

    A woman with a pale complexion and many desires, after giving birth, experienced a depletion of blood and was unaware of her surroundings. In a hurry, she treated herself with moxibustion at the qi sea for fifty-five times and subsequently recovered. She then took various tonic herbs such as ginseng, astragalus, and angelica for two months and felt stable.

    Another woman, over thirty years old, gave birth to a daughter. Two days later, she discharged something resembling a handkerchief, pointed at one end, weighing about a pound. I thought this was due to her previous exhaustion causing a qi deficiency leading to liver weakness, but fortunately her blood was not too deficient. At that time, it was the end of the year and cold, and I feared the cold would dry her out, so I promptly gave her half a qian of roasted astragalus, one qian of ginseng, five fen of white atractylodes, one and a half qian of angelica, and five fen of siler, and had her take these in three doses consecutively. She then began to sweat all over and felt at ease. However, there was a piece that fell off from the area where she was sitting, weighing about five to six taels, likely a fatty membrane.

    After eating, she was able to sleep. Upon checking her pulse, it was somewhat weak, slightly tight on the left side, and her condition appeared solid. I prescribed half a qian each of white atractylodes and peony, one qian of dried tangerine peel, and a slice of fresh ginger, simmered together for two to three doses to nourish her.[282p]

    《医学纲目》曰：一妇人产当寒月，寒气入产门，脐下胀满，手不得犯，此寒疝也。医将治之以抵当汤，谓其有瘀血也。予教之曰：非其治也。可服张仲景羊肉汤，少减作二服，遂愈。
    　　浦江吴辉妻孕时足肿，产后二日洗浴即气喘，但坐不得卧者五个月，恶风得暖稍宽，两关脉动，尺寸皆虚，百药不效，用牡丹皮、桃仁、桂枝、茯苓、干姜、枳实、厚朴、桑白皮、紫苏、五味、(艹舌)蒌仁煎汤服之即宽，二三服得卧，其痰如失。盖作污血感寒治之也。
    　　郭茂恂嫂金华君，产七日不食，始言头痛，头痛已，又心痛作，既而目睛痛如割如刺，更作更止，相去无瞬息间。每头痛甚，欲取大石压，良久渐定；心痛作，则以十指抓壁，血流满掌；痛定，目复痛，又以两手自剜取之。如是十日不已，众医无计。进黑龙丹半粒，疾少间，中夜再服，服后瞑目寝如平昔，至平旦下一行，约三升许，如蝗虫子，其疾减半；已刻又行如前，则顿愈矣。[283p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[283p]

    The "Medical Essentials" states: A woman in childbirth during the cold months, with cold air entering the birth canal, experiences distension below the navel and cannot bear to be touched; this is known as cold hernia. A physician intends to treat it with Di Dang Decoction, believing there is stagnant blood. I advised him: this is not the correct treatment. She should take a decoction of lamb from Zhang Zhongjing, slightly reducing the dose to two servings, and she was cured.

    Wu Hui's wife from Pujiang had swollen feet during pregnancy. Two days after giving birth, she experienced shortness of breath upon bathing and could not lie down for five months. She felt warm when the wind was mild. Both her pulses were weak and shallow; all medicines were ineffective. Using a decoction of peony root, peach kernel, cinnamon twig, poria, dried ginger, bitter orange, magnolia bark, mulberry white skin, perilla, and five-flavor fruit, she felt relief; after two or three doses, she could lie down, and her phlegm was expelled. This was treatment for turbid blood affected by cold.

    Guo Maoxun's sister-in-law, Lady Jinhua, did not eat for seven days post-delivery. Initially, she complained of a headache. Once that passed, she began experiencing heart pain, and then her eyes felt as if they were being cut or pierced, with the pain persisting without any moment of relief. When the headache was severe, she wanted to press a large stone on her head, and after a long time, she gradually calmed. When the heart pain came, she clawed at the wall with her fingers, causing blood to flow from her palms. When the pain subsided, her eyes hurt again, and she tried to gouge them out with her hands. For ten days, this continued, and no doctor had a solution. I gave her half a grain of Black Dragon Pill, and within a short time, during the night she took it again. After taking it, she closed her eyes and slept as usual. By dawn, she had a bowel movement of about three sheng, resembling locusts, and her ailments were reduced by half. Soon after, she returned to her previous condition and was suddenly cured.[283p]

    《薛己医案》曰：一妊妇堕胎，昏愦，不时吐痰，自用养血化痰之剂，昏愦不省，自汗发搐，痰涎涌出，彼以为中风，欲用袪风化痰。予曰：此属脾气虚寒所致，用十全大补汤加炮姜二十余剂，寻愈。
    　　一产妇阴门不闭，发热恶寒，用十全大补汤，加五味子数剂而寒热退；又用补中益气，加五味子数剂而阴户敛。若初产肿胀，或焮痛而不闭者，用加味逍遥散。若肿既消而不闭者、用补中益气汤。切忌寒凉之剂。
    　　一妊妇因产饮酒，恶露甚多，患血运，口出酒气，此血得酒热而妄行，虚而作运也。以佛手散加煨干葛二钱，一剂而痊。酒性慓悍，入月及产后不宜饮，恐致前证。产室人众，气热喧嚷，亦致此证。[284p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[284p]

    The "Xue Ji Medical Case" states: A pregnant woman had a miscarriage and was in a state of confusion, frequently spitting phlegm. She used a remedy to nourish blood and transform phlegm, but remained in a dazed state, with spontaneous sweating and convulsions, and phlegm pouring out. She thought she had a stroke and wanted to use treatments to dispel wind and transform phlegm. I said: This is caused by a deficiency and cold of the spleen. I prescribed the Shi Quan Da Bu Tang (Ten Complete Great Tonifying Decoction) with added roasted ginger for over twenty doses, and she soon recovered.

    Another postpartum woman had an open vaginal canal, experiencing fever and chills. I used the Shi Quan Da Bu Tang, adding a few doses of Wu Wei Zi (Schisandra fruit), and both chills and fever receded. Then I used Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang (Tonifying the Middle and Benefiting Qi Decoction), adding a few doses of Wu Wei Zi, which helped to close the vaginal canal. If there is initial swelling or pain and it does not close, use the Jia Wei Xiao Yao San (Modified Rambling Powder). If the swelling has subsided but it still does not close, use Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang. Avoid cold and cooling remedies.

    A pregnant woman, after drinking alcohol, had excessive lochia, suffered from blood flow issues, and exhibited a strong smell of alcohol from her mouth. This was due to the blood being heated by the alcohol and becoming erratic, moving without restraint. I prescribed the Fo Shou San (Buddha's Hand Powder) with two qian of dried kudzu, and she recovered after one dose. The nature of alcohol is fierce; consuming it during the month and after childbirth is not advisable, as it may lead to previous symptoms. A crowded delivery room, filled with hot and noisy air, can also contribute to this condition.[284p]

    一妇人产后颠狂，或用大泽兰汤而愈；后又怔忡妄言，其痰甚多，用茯苓散补其心虚顿愈；又用八珍散加远志、茯神，养其气血而瘥。
    　　一产妇亦患此证。冉化痰安神等药，病益甚，神思消沮，余以为心脾血虚不足，用大剂参、朮、芎、归、苓神、酸枣仁四斤余而安，乃以归脾汤五十余剂而愈。
    　　一产妇形体甚倦，时发讝语，用柏子仁散稍愈，又用加味归脾汤而愈。又因怒仍狂言，胁痛，小便下血，用加味逍遥散以清肝火，养肝血，顿瘥；又佐以加味归脾汤而安。
    　　一产妇不语，用七珍散而愈。后复不语，内热晡热，肢体倦怠，饮食不进，用加味归脾汤为主，佐以七珍散而愈。后因怒不语，口噤，腰背反张，手足发搐，或小便见血，面赤或青或黄，或时兼赤。余曰：面青，肝之本色也。黄者，脾气虚也。赤者，心血虚也。用八珍汤加钓藤钩、茯苓、远志渐愈，又用加味归脾汤而痊。[285p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[285p]

    A woman became delirious after childbirth; she recovered after being treated with a large dose of Ophiopogon Decoction. Later, she experienced confusion and spoke nonsensically, with excessive phlegm. A formula of Poria Powder was used to replenish her heart's deficiency, and she quickly recovered. Then, she was treated with Eight Treasure Powder plus Polygala and Poria to nourish her qi and blood, leading to healing.

    Another postpartum woman also suffered from this condition. Using herbs to transform phlegm and calm the spirit worsened her illness, causing more depletion in her thoughts and spirit. I believed it was due to insufficient heart-spleen blood, so I prescribed a large dose of ginseng, atractylodes, chuanxiong, angelica, and Poria, along with sour jujube seeds, totaling more than four catties for nourishment. Subsequently, she was treated with more than fifty doses of Gui Pi Decoction and recovered.

    One woman felt extremely fatigued and occasionally spoke gibberish. She improved slightly with Bai Zi Ren Powder, and was cured with a modified Gui Pi Decoction. She later became angry and again spoke nonsensically, suffered from flank pain, and experienced blood in her urine. I used a modified Xiao Yao San to clear liver fire and nourish liver blood, resulting in rapid healing. This was supplemented with modified Gui Pi Decoction to provide further stability.

    A postpartum woman who was mute was treated with Qi Zhen San and recovered. Later, she became mute again, experiencing internal heat and afternoon fever, fatigue, and an inability to eat. I used modified Gui Pi Decoction as the main treatment, supplemented with Qi Zhen San, leading to her recovery. Later, due to anger, she remained silent, with her mouth shut, her back arched, hands and feet twitching, occasional blood in her urine, and facial discoloration—either red, blue, or yellow, or sometimes a combination. I noted: a blue face indicates liver issues; yellow indicates spleen deficiency; red indicates heart blood deficiency. I treated her with Eight Treasure Decoction, adding Uncaria and Poria, which resulted in gradual improvement, followed by modified Gui Pi Decoction for her complete recovery.[285p]

    一产妇产后乍见鬼神，或用调经散愈而复作，仍服前散益甚，痰涎上涌，朝寒暮热，余朝用八珍散，夕用加味归脾汤，各五十余剂而愈。
    　　一妇人产后心神惊悸，初度服琥珀地黄丸、局方妙香散，随效。再患服之，其证益甚而脉浮大，按之如无，发热恶寒，此血气俱空虚，余用十全大补、加味归脾二汤各百余剂而愈。后遇惊恐，劳怒复作，仍服前药而安。
    　　一妇人产后中风，心惊不省人事，言语妄甚，恶风寒，喜热饮，形气倦怠，脉虚浮无力。余谓血气虚寒，用十全大补汤二十余剂，不应；又二十余剂，稍缓；力渐加附子至一钱，服数剂，诸证减一二。又二十余剂，十退三四；乃去附子五分，数剂，诸证顿退而安。后又发，仍服前药，加附子三五分而愈。[286p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[286p]

    A woman experienced ghostly apparitions after childbirth. She initially used a menstrual regulation powder, which improved her condition but then worsened again. The symptoms intensified, with phlegm rising, morning chills, and evening fevers. I prescribed Ba Zhen San in the morning and Jia Wei Gui Pi Decoction in the evening, administering over fifty doses, and she recovered.

    Another woman, distressed after giving birth, suffered from anxiety and palpitations. Initially, she took Amber Rehmannia Pills and a specific aromatic powder, both of which helped. However, after further treatment, her symptoms worsened, with a large floating pulse that felt absent upon palpation, along with fever and chills. This indicated a deficiency of both blood and qi. I used Shi Quan Da Bu Decoction and Jia Wei Gui Pi Decoction, each over a hundred doses, and she improved. Later, when she experienced fright or anger, the symptoms reappeared, and she found relief with the previous medicines.

    Another woman suffered a stroke after childbirth, presenting with extreme fright, confusion, incoherent speech, an aversion to wind and cold, a desire for hot drinks, and general fatigue with a weak floating pulse. I concluded that her blood and qi were deficient and cold, prescribing over twenty doses of Shi Quan Da Bu Decoction, which yielded no results; after another twenty doses, she showed slight improvement. I gradually increased the dosage of Aconite until it reached a quarter, and after several doses, her symptoms reduced somewhat. Following an additional twenty doses, three or four symptoms receded. I then reduced Aconite by five grams, and after several doses, she experienced significant relief and stability. Later, when symptoms resurfaced, I prescribed the same previous formula, adding Aconite by three to five grams, resulting in her recovery.[286p]

    一妇人产后中风，恍惚盗汗自汗，发热晡热，面色黄白，四肢畏冷，此气血俱虚，用八珍汤不应，更用十全大补、加味归脾二汤始应。后因劳怒，发厥昏愦，左目牵紧，两唇抽动，小便自遗，余以为肝火炽盛，用十全大补加钓藤、山栀而安，再用十全大补汤、辰砂远志丸而愈。
    　　一妇人产后虚极生风，或用诸补剂，四肢逆冷自汗，泄泻肠鸣腹痛，余以阳气虚寒，用六君子，姜、附各加至五钱，不应；以参、附各十两，始应。良久不服，仍肠鸣腹痛，后灸关元六百余壮，及服十全大补汤方效。
    　　一产妇略闻音响，其汗如水而昏愦，诸药到口即呕，余以为脾气虚散，用参附末为细丸，时衔三五粒，随液咽下，乃渐加之至钱许，却服参附汤而痊。[287p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[287p]

    A woman suffered a stroke after childbirth, exhibiting symptoms such as confusion, sweating with a feeling of heat, and a pale yellow complexion. Her limbs were cold, indicating a deficiency in both qi and blood. The use of Ba Zhen Tang was ineffective, so I prescribed Shi Quan Da Bu Tang along with a modified Gui Pi San, which began to take effect. Later, due to overexertion and anger, she experienced seizures, confusion, spasms in her left eye, twitching of her lips, and involuntary urination. I believed this was due to excess liver fire, so I added Diaoteng and Shanzhi to the Shi Quan Da Bu Tang, which calmed her down. Lastly, I administered Shi Quan Da Bu Tang and Chen Sha Yuan Zhi Wan, and she recovered.

    Another woman experienced extreme deficiency after childbirth, leading to wind. She had tried various tonics but her limbs were cold, she was sweating, suffered from diarrhea, and had abdominal pain. I diagnosed a deficiency of yang qi and prescribed Liu Jun Zi Tang, increasing the amounts of ginger and Aconite to five qian. This was ineffective, so I used ten liang each of ginseng and Aconite, which finally helped. After a long period without improvement, with persistent abdominal pain and rumbling, I performed moxibustion on Guanyuan over six hundred times, and eventually, she responded to Shi Quan Da Bu Tang.

    A postpartum woman experienced intense sweating and confusion upon hearing a sound, and she would vomit any medicine placed in her mouth. I suspected her spleen qi was scattered, so I made fine pills from ginseng and Aconite powder, instructing her to take three to five grains at a time with liquid. Gradually, I increased it to about one qian, at which point she could tolerate taking Shi Quan Da Bu Tang and recovered.[287p]

    一产妇盗汗不止，遂致废寐、神思疲甚，口干引饮，余谓血虚有热，用当归补血汤以代茶，又以当归六黄汤，黄芩、连、蘗炒黑，倍加人参、五味子，二剂而愈。
    　　一产妇牙关紧急，腰背反张，四肢抽搐，两目连札，此去血过多，元气亏损、阴火炽盛，用十全大补加炮姜一剂而苏，数剂而安。
    　　余在吴江史万湖第时，将入，更闻喧嚷，询云：家人妇产后出直厨，忽仆而死，余意其劳伤气血而发痉也。急用十全大补加附子煎滚，令人正其面，开其口灌之，久不能下；令侧其面而出之，换以热药，如此五次，方得下咽，遂苏。
    　　一产妇勤于女工，忽仆地，牙关紧急，痰喘气粗，四肢不遂，此气血虚而发痉，朝用补中益气汤加茯苓、半夏，夕用八珍汤加半夏各三十余剂，不应；此气血之未复，药之未及也，仍用前二汤，又五十余剂而痊。
    　　一产妇筋挛臂软，肌肉掣动，此气血俱虚而自热也，用十全大补汤而安。[288p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[288p]

    A woman after childbirth had continuous sweating, leading to sleeplessness and extreme fatigue, with a dry mouth urging her to drink. I believed this indicated a deficiency of blood with internal heat, so I used a blood-replenishing decoction with angelica in place of tea, and also prescribed a modified Tang Gui Liu Huang Decoction with Huang Qin, Lian, and Bi Yan roasted black, increasing the amounts of Ren Shen and Wu Wei Zi. After two doses, she recovered.

    Another woman after childbirth had her jaws tightly clenched, her back arched, limbs twitching, and her eyes rolling. This was due to excessive blood loss, depletion of vital energy, and fiery Yin. I used a dose of Shi Quan Da Bu Tang with roasted ginger, and she revived after several doses.

    When I was at Shi Wan Hu in Wujiang, I heard a commotion as I was about to enter. Inquiring, I learned that a woman in labor had suddenly collapsed and died. I suspected labor had exhausted her energy and blood, causing convulsions. I urgently administered Shi Quan Da Bu Tang with Fu Zi, boiling it down and having someone properly position her, opening her mouth to pour it in. She couldn't swallow for a long time, so I turned her to her side to let it out and replaced it with hot medicine. After five times of this, she finally swallowed and revived.

    One woman, diligent in her needlework, suddenly collapsed, had clenched jaws, gasped for air, and her limbs were nonresponsive. This was convulsion due to deficiency in energy and blood. In the morning, I used a nurturing middle and benefiting Qi decoction with Fu Ling and Ban Xia, and in the evening, I used Ba Zhen Tang with Ban Xia, with more than thirty doses, but it was ineffective; the energy and blood had not yet recovered, and the medicine had not taken effect. I continued with the previous two decoctions and after more than fifty doses, she was cured.

    Another postpartum woman experienced muscle spasms with weak arms, her muscles twitching. This was due to deficiency of both energy and blood, leading to self-induced heat. I used Shi Quan Da Bu Tang, and she was at ease.[288p]

    一产妇两手麻木，服愈风丹天麻丸，遍身皆麻，神思倦怠，晡热作渴，自汗盗汗。余谓气血俱虚，用十全大补汤数剂，诸证悉退，又数剂而全愈。但内热，用加味逍遥散而痊。
    　　一产妇因劳而臂不能屈，服苏合香丸，肢体痿软，汗出如水。余谓前药辛香，耗散真气，腠理虚而津液妄泄也。先用十全大补汤加五味子，补实腠理，收敛真气，汗顿止；又佐以四君子调补元气渐愈，用逍遥散大补汤调理而痊。
    　　一产妇先胸胁乳内胀痛，后因怒，口噤吐痰，臂不能伸，小便自遗，左三部脉弦，余谓此肝经血虚而风火所致。不能养筋，先用加味逍遥散治之，臂能屈伸；又以补肝散六味丸，诸证悉愈。
    　　一妇人发瘈，遗尿自汗，面赤或时面青，饮食如故，肝脉弦紧。余曰：此肝经血燥风热，名瘈也。肝主小便，其色青，入心则赤。法当滋阴血，清肝火，遂用加味逍遥散，不数剂诸证悉退而安。[289p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[289p]

    A woman in childbirth experienced numbness in her hands. After taking Yufeng Dan and Tianma Wan, her whole body became numb, her spirit felt weary, she had evening heat and thirst, and she experienced spontaneous sweating and night sweats. I believed that both her qi and blood were deficient, so I prescribed several doses of Shiquan Dabu Tang, and all her symptoms subsided; after a few more doses, she fully recovered. However, due to internal heat, I used Jiamwei Xiaoyao San for her full recovery.

    Another woman in childbirth, due to exertion, could not bend her arms. After taking Suhe Xiang Wan, her limbs felt weak, and she sweated profusely. I believed that this previous medicine was spicy and aromatic, which depletes vital energy, causing her skin pores to be weak and fluids to leak out. First, I used Shiquan Dabu Tang with Wuweizi to nourish and strengthen the skin’s pores and consolidate vital energy, and her sweating suddenly stopped. I then supplemented with Sijunzi Tang to gradually restore her original qi, and she recovered completely.

    One woman had initial swelling and pain in her chest and sides, and later, due to anger, she became mute, vomited phlegm, could not extend her arms, and had urinary incontinence. The left three regions of her pulse were taut. I believed that this was caused by deficiency of blood in the liver meridian, leading to wind and fire issues. Unable to nourish her tendons, I first used Jiamwei Xiaoyao San to treat her, and she was able to bend and extend her arms; then, I used Buliangan San Liuwuwei Wan, and all her symptoms were resolved.

    One woman had a condition called "zhì" with urinary incontinence, spontaneous sweating, a red face, and occasionally a blue face, while her appetite remained the same; her liver pulse was taut and tight. I said: This is caused by dryness of blood and wind-heat in the liver meridian, referred to as "zhì." The liver governs urination, and its color is blue, which turns red when it enters the heart. The treatment should nourish yin and blood while clearing liver fire. Therefore, I used Jiamwei Xiaoyao San, and after several doses, all her symptoms subsided, and she became stable.[289p]

    一产妇患脚气，或用独活寄生汤而痊；后复作服之，其汗如水，更加口噤吐痰。余用十全大补汤，培养血气渐愈。后饮食日少，肌体目瘦，吐痰如涌，此命门火衰，脾土虚寒，用八味丸及加味归脾汤，诸证渐退，肌肉渐生。
    　　一产妇身腹作痛，发热不食，烦躁不寐，盗汗胁痛，服解散袪血之药，不时昏愦，六脉洪大如无，用补中益气加炮姜、半夏一剂，顿退二三；又一剂，寝食甘美，但背强而痛；用八珍散、大补汤调理而安。
    　　一产妇遍身头项作痛，恶寒拘急，脉浮紧，此风寒之证也，用五积散一剂，汗出而愈。但倦怠发热，此邪气去而真气虚也，用八珍汤调补而痊。
    　　一产妇腰痛腹胀善噫，诸药皆呕，余以为脾虚血弱，用白朮一味炒黄，每剂一两，米泔煎，时饮匙许，四剂后渐安，百余剂而愈。[290p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[290p]

    A postpartum woman suffered from athlete’s foot, which healed with the use of the Du Huo Ji Sheng Decoction; however, it recurred upon reapplication, leading to profuse sweating and increased phlegm with mouth closure. I used the Shi Quan Da Bu Decoction to nurture her blood and energy, and she gradually improved. Later, her appetite diminished, her body became emaciated, and she experienced abundant phlegm, indicating a decline in the fire of life and a deficiency of cold in the spleen. I administered the Ba Wei Pill and an enhanced Gui Pi Decoction, and her symptoms gradually receded, while her flesh started to grow.

    A postpartum woman experienced pain in her body and abdomen, fever, loss of appetite, restlessness, insomnia, night sweat, and pain in the side. She took medicine to resolve blood stasis but often fell into a faint state, and her six pulse positions were large and weak. I prescribed a dose of the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Decoction with roasted ginger and Ban Xia, which brought relief. After another dose, she could eat and sleep well, though her back remained sore. I then used the Ba Zhen Powder and the Da Bu Decoction for balance and comfort.

    A postpartum woman had widespread head and neck pain, felt cold and tense, and had a floating and tight pulse, indicating a pattern of wind and cold. I administered a dose of the Wu Ji San, which induced sweating and brought recovery. However, she then felt fatigued and feverish, suggesting that the evil qi had departed but the true qi was still weak. I treated her with the Ba Zhen Decoction for nourishment, leading to her full recovery.

    A postpartum woman experienced low back pain, abdominal bloating, and excessive belching, with all medicines causing vomiting. I suspected a deficiency of the spleen and weakness of blood, so I used only roasted Atractylodes, one tael per dose, decocted with rice wash, recommending she drink a spoonful periodically. After four doses, she gradually felt settled, eventually requiring over a hundred doses for complete recovery.[290p]

    一产妇恶露淋沥，体倦面黄，食少恶寒，昼夜不寐，惊悸汗出，此脾经虚热，用加味归脾汤而痊。后因怒胁胀，作呕少食，用六君子加柴胡治之而痊。
    　　一产妇恶露不下，服竣厉之剂，恶露随下，久而昏愦，以手护其腹。余日：此脾气复伤作痛，故用手护也。以人参理中汤加肉桂二剂，补之而愈。
    　　一产妇恶露上攻，心痛昏愦口噤，冷汗不止，手足厥逆，用六君子加附子一钱以回其阳，二剂顿苏；又以十全大补汤养其血气而安。
    　　一产妇患前证，手不敢近腹，用失笑散一服，下瘀血而愈。次日复痛，亦用前药而安。
    　　一产妇患前证，用大黄等药，其血虽下，复患头痛，发热恶寒，次日昏愦，自以两手坚护其腹，不得诊脉，视其面色青白，知为脾气虚寒而痛也，用六君子汤加姜、桂而痛止，又用八珍汤加姜、桂调理而安。
    　　一妇产后。小腹痛甚，牙关紧急，此瘀血内停，灌以失笑散下血而苏，又用四物加炮姜、自朮、陈皮而愈。[291p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[291p]

    A postpartum woman experienced prolonged lochia, felt weak and looked pale, had little appetite and was sensitive to cold, remained sleepless day and night, and experienced palpitations and sweating. This indicated deficiency heat in the spleen channel. Using a modified Gui Pi Decoction, she recovered. Later, due to anger, she suffered from abdominal distension, nausea, and reduced food intake, which was treated with a modified Liu Jun Zi Decoction, resulting in recovery.

    Another postpartum woman had lochia that did not discharge. After taking an astringent medicine, the lochia began to flow, but she became dizzy and protected her abdomen with her hands. I pointed out: This was due to injury to the spleen qi causing pain, hence she guarded it. A treatment of Ren Shen Li Zhong Decoction with two doses of cinnamon was given to replenish her, and she recovered.

    A postpartum woman experienced lochia rising upward, heart pain, dizziness, locked jaw, and persistent cold sweat, with limbs pale and cold. A modified Liu Jun Zi Decoction with one qian of Fu Zi was used to revive her yang, and she quickly regained consciousness with two doses. Additionally, a Shi Quan Da Bu Decoction was used to nourish her blood and qi, promoting stability.

    A postpartum woman suffered from the previous symptoms and was afraid to touch her abdomen. I administered a dose of Shi Xiao San, which expelled the blood stasis and cured her. The next day, she had pain again and was treated with the same medicine to achieve relief.

    Another postpartum woman had similar symptoms, took Da Huang and other medicines, and though the blood discharged, she later experienced a headache, fever, and chills. The next day, she felt dizzy and used both hands to firmly protect her abdomen, making pulse diagnosis impossible. Observing her pale complexion, it was discerned that she had deficiency cold in the spleen causing pain. A Liu Jun Zi Decoction with ginger and cinnamon was administered to alleviate the pain. Additionally, Ba Zhen Decoction with ginger and cinnamon was applied to regulate her condition, leading to stability.

    One woman postpartum had severe lower abdominal pain and tight jaw, indicating stasis of blood within. Shixiao San was used to dispel the blood and she revived. A further treatment of Si Wu Decoction with toasted ginger, Atractylodes, and dried tangerine peel resulted in her recovery.[291p]

    一妇人经水来，比常度过多不止，遂服濇药止之，致腹作痛，此乃气血凝滞也，用失笑散二服而愈。
    　　一产妇儿枕腹痛，或用驱逐之剂，昏愦口噤，手足发搐，此血气虚极之变证也，用八珍汤加炮姜二钱四剂，未应；又以十全大补汤，加炮姜二钱，二剂而苏。
    　　一产妇小腹疼痛，小便不利，用薏苡仁汤二剂痛止，更以四物加红花、桃仁下瘀血而愈。大抵此证皆因荣卫不调，或瘀血停滞所致。若脉洪数，已有脓脉。但数微有脓，脉迟紧乃瘀血，下之即愈，若腹胀大，转侧作水声，或脓从脐出，或从大便出，宜用蜡矾丸、太乙膏及托里药。
    　　一产妇恶露停滞，小腹患痛，服瓜子仁汤，下瘀血而痊。凡瘀血停滞，宜急治之，缓则腐化为脓，最难治疗。若流注关节则患骨疽，失治多为败证。
    　　一产妇恶露停滞，小腹作痛有块，脉芤而濇，以四物加延胡、红花、桃仁、牛膝、木香治之而愈。[292p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[292p]

    A woman experienced an excessive menstrual flow compared to her usual amount and took a medicine to stop it, which led to abdominal pain. This was due to stagnation of qi and blood; using the Shixiao Powder twice resolved the issue.

    A postpartum woman felt pain in her abdomen and head, and after taking a driving-away medicine became dazed and unable to speak, with convulsions in her hands and feet. This was a severe transformation of qi and blood deficiency; using the Eight Treasure Decoction with two qian of blast-fried ginger in four doses initially did not help. Afterward, the Complete Ginseng Decoction with two qian of blast-fried ginger in two doses revived her.

    A postpartum woman had abdominal pain and difficulty urinating. Taking Coix Seed Decoction twice relieved the pain. It was further treated with Four Substance Decoction plus safflower and peach kernel to remove stagnant blood, leading to recovery. Generally, these symptoms arise from disordered nourishing and guarding forces, or from stagnant blood. If the pulse is rapid, there is already a purulent pulse. However, if the pulse is weak with some purulence and is slow and tense, it indicates stagnant blood; purging will resolve it. If there is abdominal distension and turning over produces a watery sound, or if pus comes out through the navel or stool, it is advisable to use wax alum pills, Taiyi ointment, and medicines for dredging.

    A postpartum woman had stagnant lochia, abdominal pain, and there were clots. The pulse was hollow and rapid. Using Four Substance Decoction with cyperus, safflower, peach kernel, ox knee, and patchouli cured her.[292p]

    王时亨室产后腰间肿痛，两腿尤甚，此由瘀血滞于经络而然也。不早治，必作骨疽。逐与桃仁汤二剂稍愈，更以没药丸数服而痊。
    　　汪中翰侧室，产后小腹作痛，诸药不应，其脉洪滑且数，此瘀血内溃为脓也，以瓜子仁汤痛止，更以太乙膏下脓而愈。今人产后多有此病，纵非痈患，用之更效。有人脐出脓水，久而不愈，亦以前膏及蜡矾丸治之，亦愈。
    　　一产妇腹中似有一块，或时作痛而转动，按之不痛，面色痿黄，痛则皎白，脉浮而濇，余谓此肝气虚而血弱也。不信，乃用破血行气之药，痛益甚，转动无常。又认以为血鳖，再用破血袪逐之药，痛攻两胁，肚腹尤甚。益信为鳖确，服下虫等药，去血甚多，形气愈虚，肢节间各结小核，隐于肉里，以为鳖子畏药而走于外。余云：肝藏血而养诸筋，此因肝血复损，筋涸而挛结耳。盖肢节胸项，皆属肝胆部分，养其肝木，补金水以滋肝血，则筋自舒。遂用八珍汤、逍遥散、归脾汤加减，调治而愈。[293p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[293p]

    Wang Shiheng experienced swelling and pain in the lower back after giving birth, particularly severe in both legs. This was due to stagnant blood blocking the meridians. If not treated early, it would inevitably lead to a bone abscess. He was given two doses of Taoren Decoction, which brought slight improvement, and then took several doses of Myrrh Pill and fully recovered.

    Wang Zhonghan's wife had abdominal pain after childbirth, and none of the medicines worked. Her pulse was surging and slippery, and rapid; this indicated that stagnant blood had turned into pus. Guaziren Decoction relieved the pain, and further treatment with Taiyi Ointment expelled the pus, leading to recovery. Many modern women experience this condition after childbirth; even if it is not an abscess, using such treatments is still effective. There were cases where a woman's navel discharged pus for a long time without healing, which was also treated with the ointment and wax alum pill, resulting in recovery.

    One postpartum woman felt as if there were a mass in her abdomen, which sometimes caused pain when moved; it did not hurt when pressed. She had a sallow complexion, and when in pain, her face turned pale and white. Her pulse was floating and thin. I believed this indicated deficient liver qi and weak blood. Doubting my diagnosis, she used medicines to break blood and promote qi, which only worsened the pain and caused irregular movements. She further suspected it to be a blood tumor and used more aggressive blood-dispersing medicines, which led to severe pain in the sides and abdomen. Believing it to be a tumor, she took parasitic medicines, which resulted in a significant loss of blood, making her vitality even weaker, along with small nodules forming in the joints, hidden in the flesh, leading her to think the tumor was evading medication. I stated: the liver stores blood and nourishes the tendons; this condition arose from the depletion of liver blood, causing the tendons to become tight. The arms and joints, as well as the chest and neck, are associated with the liver and gallbladder. Nourishing the liver wood, and supplementing gold and water to enrich liver blood, would ease the tendons. I then prescribed Eight Treasures Decoction, Xiaoyao Powder, and Guipi Decoction, adjusted appropriately, and successfully brought about recovery.[293p]

    一妇人寒月中产后腹大痛，觉有块，百方不治。一人教以羊肉四两，熟地黄二两，生姜一两，水煎服之，二三次愈。
    　　一产妇小腹作痛，小便不利，内热晡热，形体倦怠，余用加味逍遥散以清肝火、生肝血，用补中益气汤补脾胃、升阳气而痊。
    　　一产妇小腹痛，或作呕，或昏愦，此脾气虚寒，用人参理中汤渐愈，又以补中益气汤加茯苓、半夏痊愈。后复作痛而兼喘，仍用补中益气汤培补脾肺而瘥。
    　　一产妇患腹胀，或用抵当汤，败血已下，前证益甚，小腹重坠，似欲去后。余谓此脾气虚而下陷，用补中益气汤加炮姜温补脾气，重坠如失；又用六君子汤而安。[294p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[294p]

    A woman, after giving birth in the cold month, experienced severe abdominal pain and felt a mass, and no remedies worked for her. Someone advised her to take four taels of lamb, two taels of cooked rehmannia, and one tael of fresh ginger, boiled in water to drink it, and after two or three doses, she recovered.

    A postpartum woman experienced lower abdominal pain and had difficulty urinating, accompanied by internal heat and fatigue. I used a modified Xiaoyao Powder to clear the liver fire and nourish the liver blood and employed the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Decoction to tonify the spleen and stomach, raising the vital energy, which led to her recovery.

    Another postpartum woman had abdominal pain, with symptoms of vomiting or dizziness. This was due to deficiency and cold in the spleen. I gradually treated her with Ren Shen Li Zhong Decoction and further administered the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Decoction with added poria and pinellia, leading to her recovery. However, she later had renewed pain along with wheezing; I continued with the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Decoction to nourished the spleen and lungs, and she healed.

    A postpartum woman suffered from abdominal distension. I used the Di Dang Decoction, and after blood had been expelled, the previous symptoms intensified. She felt heavy and had a sensation of something wanting to come out. I assessed her condition as a deficiency of spleen qi leading to sinking. I administered the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Decoction with added roasted ginger to warm and tonify the spleen qi, alleviating the heaviness. I also used the Liu Jun Zi Decoction for stabilization.[294p]

    一产妇因怒，两胁胀痛，吐血甚多，发热恶寒，胸腹胀痛，余以为气血俱虚，用八珍加柴胡、丹皮、炮姜而血顿止，又用十全大补汤而寒热渐退。此证苟非用姜、桂辛温，助脾肺以行药势，不惟无以施其功，而反助其胀耳。
    　　一产妇恶寒发热，余以为血气虚寒，用十全大补加炮姜而寒热愈；但饮食不甘，肢体倦怠，用补中益气而肢体安。又食后犯怒，恶寒发热，抽搐咬牙，面色青中隐黄，欲按其腹，以手护之，此肝木侮脾土，饮食停滞，用六君子加木香一剂而安。
    　　一产妇恶寒发热，余以为气血俱虚，欲用八珍加炮姜治之。其家知医，以为风寒，用小柴胡汤，致汗出讝语，烦热作渴，肢体抽搐。余曰：寒热不时，乃气血虚。用十全大补二剂，益甚，脉洪大，重按如无，此药力不能及，乃加附子，四剂稍缓，数剂而安。[295p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[295p]

    A postpartum woman experienced severe anger, pain in her sides, excessive vomiting of blood, fever, and chills, along with swelling and pain in her chest and abdomen. I believed this was due to a deficiency of both qi and blood, so I used Ba Zhen (Eight Treasures Decoction) with the addition of Chai Hu (Bupleurum) and Dan Pi (Moutan Bark). The bleeding stopped immediately, and I then administered Shi Quan Da Bu Tang (Ten Complements Greatly Augmenting Decoction), which gradually alleviated her chills and fever. If it were not for the use of ginger, cinnamon, and other spicy warm herbs to support the spleen and lungs to facilitate the effects of the medicine, it would not have worked, but instead, it would have exacerbated her distention.

    Another postpartum woman experienced chills and fever. I believed she had a deficiency of blood and coldness, so I used Shi Quan Da Bu Tang with ginger, which improved her chills and fever. However, her appetite was poor, and she felt fatigued. I then administered Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang (Supplement the Middle and Benefit Qi Decoction) to help restore her vitality. Later, after eating, she became angry, experienced chills and fever, had convulsions and grinding of the teeth, and her complexion showed a greenish hue with a hint of yellow. She wanted to protect her abdomen with her hands. This indicated that the liver wood was injuring the spleen earth, leading to stagnation of food intake; I prescribed Liu Jun Zi Tang (Six Gentlemen Decoction) with a dose of Mu Xiang (Aucklandia) to calm her.

    In another case, a postpartum woman experienced chills and fever. I believed it was a deficiency of both qi and blood and intended to treat her with Ba Zhen Decoction and ginger. However, her family, knowledgeable in medicine, assumed it was caused by wind-cold and insisted on using Xiao Chai Hu Tang (Minor Bupleurum Decoction), resulting in sweating and incoherent speech, with restlessness and thirst, along with body convulsions. I stated that the intermittent chills and fever indicated a deficiency of qi and blood. By using two doses of Shi Quan Da Bu Tang, her condition improved significantly; however, her pulse was large and forceful, and palpation indicated a lack of response, meaning this medicine wasn't sufficient. I then added Fu Zi (Aconite) into four doses to slightly alleviate her condition, and after a few more doses, she stabilized.[295p]

    一产妇朝寒暮热，或不时寒热，久不愈，用六君子补中益气，兼服百余剂而寻愈。
    　　大尹余君之内，产后发热晡热，吐血便血兼盗汗，小便频数，胸胁胀痛，肚腹痞闷。余曰：此诸脏虚损也，证当固本为善。自恃知医，用降火之剂，更加泻利肠鸣，呕吐不食，腹痛足冷，始信余言。诊其脉，或浮洪，或沉细，或如无，其面或青黄，或赤白，此虚寒假热之状，时虽仲夏，当舍时从证，先用六君子加炮姜、肉桂数剂，胃气渐复，诸证渐退；更佐以十全大补汤半载而痊愈。
    　　儒者杨敬之内人所患同前，但吐痰涎，或用温补化痰之剂不应，面色黧黑，两尺浮大，按之微细，此因命门火虚，不能生脾土，脾土不能生诸脏而为患也，用八味丸补土之母而痊。[296p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[296p]

    A woman after childbirth experienced alternating chills and fever, occasionally suffering from both, and remained unwell for a long time. By using the Six Gentlemen Decoction to tonify the middle and enhance energy, and taking more than a hundred doses, she eventually recovered.

    In the case of a high official’s wife, after giving birth, she had fevers in the evening, coughed up blood, had blood in her stool, experienced night sweats, frequently urinated, and felt chest and side pain, along with abdominal distension. I said: this is the result of deficiency in the organs; the approach should focus on strengthening the foundations. Relying on self-knowledge of medicine, she used heat-clearing remedies and her condition worsened, leading to diarrhea, audible bowel sounds, vomiting, loss of appetite, abdominal pain, and cold feet. She began to trust my words. Upon pulse diagnosis, it was either floating and surging, or sinking and thin, or almost imperceptible; her complexion varied between dark yellow and red white, indicating a condition of false heat due to vacuity and coldness. Although it was mid-summer, I adhered to the diagnosis and first used the Six Gentlemen Decoction with additional roasted ginger and cinnamon for a few doses. Gradually her gastric energy recovered and the symptoms diminished. Afterward, I complemented the treatment with the Complete Tonifying Decoction for half a year until she fully recovered.

    The wife of scholar Yang Jing had similar symptoms, but primarily coughed up phlegm. She had used warming and tonifying remedies to transform phlegm, but they were ineffective. Her complexion was dark and her pulse was floating and large in the two inches, but weak upon palpation. This was due to the deficiency of fire in the life gate, unable to generate the spleen earth; the spleen earth then was unable to nourish the organs, leading to her condition. I used the Eight Treasures Pill to tonify the mother of the earth and she was cured.[296p]

    一产妇食角黍，烦渴痞闷，腹痛，大便欲去不去，服消导等药不应，饮食日减，肌体日瘦半月矣。余谓此食积为患，用大酒曲炒为末，温酒调服二钱，俄间腹鸣良久，仍下粽而愈。
    　　一产妇食鱼鲊，腹痛患痢，诸药不应，用陈皮、白朮等分为末，陈皮汤送下，数服而愈。
    　　一产妇患血渴，朝寒暮热，肚腹作痛，以手按之不痛，余以为血气俱虚，用八珍之类治之。彼反行逐血，更加发热烦躁，余用当归补血渴，热躁渐止；用八珍、麦门、五味，气血渐复。
    　　一妇人每产后，齿龈皆动，逾日乃止，此气血虚而火动也。后复怀妊，临月付十全大补汤二剂，令产后煎服，其齿不动如故。
    　　一妇人产后喉痛，服清热等剂，痛益甚，此膀胱经血虚也，盖膀胱之经脉上行至喉而还。用八珍汤加牡丹皮、柴胡、酒炒黑黄蘗二剂而愈。[297p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[297p]

    A woman in labor ate glutinous millet, experiencing thirst, discomfort, and abdominal pain, with an inability to defecate despite taking digestive medicines. Her appetite diminished daily, and she lost weight over half a month. I diagnosed it as food accumulation causing problems. I ground large wine yeast into a powder, mixed it with warm wine, and had her take two qian. Soon, her abdomen gurgled for a long time, and she eventually had a bowel movement, resulting in recovery.

    Another woman ate salted fish and suffered abdominal pain and dysentery, with various medicines proving ineffective. I used a powdered mixture of dried tangerine peel and white atractylodes, administered with a decoction of dried tangerine peel, and she recovered after taking it multiple times.

    A woman suffered from thirst with cold in the morning and heat in the evening, accompanied by abdominal pain that didn’t hurt when pressed. I believed it was due to deficiency of blood and qi, treating her with Eight Treasures medicinal formula. However, she insisted on expelling blood, which intensified her heat and agitation. I then used angelica to nourish her blood from the thirst, and the heat and restlessness gradually subsided; I combined Eight Treasures with wheat-spike and five-flavor herbs, and her qi and blood slowly improved.

    A woman experienced loose gums after each childbirth, with the condition persisting for days. This was due to deficiency of both qi and blood causing fire movement. Later, when she became pregnant again, I prescribed two doses of a comprehensive nourishing soup for the last month of her pregnancy, which ensured her gums remained stable after childbirth.

    Another woman had a sore throat after giving birth. She took cooling medicines, but the pain worsened. This was due to deficiency of blood in the bladder channel, as the bladder meridian ascends the throat and then returns. I used the Eight Treasures soup and added tree peony root, bupleurum, and wine-fried black yellow gentian in two doses, and she recovered.[297p]

    一妇人性善怒，产后唇肿内热，或用清热败毒散，唇口肿胀，日晡热甚，月水不调；再用降火化痰，遂令食少作呕，大便不实，唇出血水；又用理气消导，胸膈痞满，头目不清，唇肿经闭；又甩清胃行血，肢体倦怠，发热烦躁，涎水涌出；又欲用通经之剂。余曰：病本七情，肝经亏损，又数行攻伐，故元气益虚也，法当补阴益阳，遂以济生归脾汤、加味逍遥散、补中益气汤调治，元气渐复，诸证悉愈，后因怒，寒热耳痛，胸膈胀闷，唇焮肿甚，此是怒动肝火所伤，遂用四物合小柴胡加山栀顿愈。后又怒，胁乳作胀，肚腹作内，呕吐酸涎，饮食不入，小水不利，此是怒动肝木克脾土，乃用补脾气番脾血而愈。又因劳役怒气，饮食失时，发热喘渴，体倦不食，去血如崩，唇肿炽甚，此是肝经有火，脾经气虚，遂月补中益气加炒黑山栀、芍药、丹皮而愈。此证每见，但治其疮，不固其本，而死者多矣。[298p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[298p]

    A woman had a good nature but was prone to anger. After giving birth, her lips became swollen and there was internal heat. At times, she used a cooling formula to eliminate toxins, but her lips continued to swell, and she experienced increased heat in the late afternoon, along with irregular menstrual cycles. When she tried a remedy to reduce fire and phlegm, it resulted in decreased appetite, nausea, and loose stools with blood in her lips. Then, she used a remedy to regulate blood flow, but she felt chest tightness, dizziness, and swelling of the lips with menstrual irregularities.

    Next, she attempted to clear the stomach and promote blood flow, which caused body fatigue, fever, irritability, and excessive salivation. She also wanted to use a formula to open her menstrual flow. I remarked that her illness stemmed from emotional issues, with damage to her liver Qi from repeated stress, leading to further depletion of her vital energy. The treatment should focus on nourishing Yin and enhancing Yang. Thus, I prescribed the Ji Sheng Gui Pi Decoction, a modified version of the Xiao Yao San, and the Bu Zhong Yi Qi Decoction. Gradually, her vital energy recovered, and all her symptoms improved.

    Later, due to another outburst of anger, she experienced cold and hot ear pain, chest tightness, and severe swelling of the lips. This was caused by anger stirring up liver fire, so I administered Si Wu Decoction combined with Xiao Chai Hu Decoction, adding mountain gardenia, and she quickly recovered. Afterward, she became angry again, which led to swelling in her sides and breasts, abdominal distension, and the production of sour vomit, along with difficulty eating and painful urination. This indicated that her anger was affecting the liver's ability to support the spleen; thus, I used a treatment to replenish spleen Qi and nourish spleen blood, leading to her recovery.

    After suffering from labor and anger, along with irregular eating patterns, she experienced fever, shortness of breath, thirst, fatigue, loss of appetite, and severe swelling of the lips, which indicated fire in her liver meridian and Qi deficiency in her spleen. Therefore, I prescribed a monthly dose of Bu Zhong Yi Qi, adding roasted black mountain gardenia, peony, and moutan root, resulting in her recovery. Such cases are often seen; however, treating only the symptoms without addressing the root causes has led to many deaths.[298p]

    一膏梁之妇，产后月经不调，唇裂焮肿，内热殊甚，服寒凉之剂，后不时出水，余用加味清胃散而愈。后值春令，兼怒，唇口肿胀，寒热作呕，痰甚少，用小柴胡加山栀、茯苓、桔梗，诸证顿愈；但内热仍作，乃以加味逍遥散调理而安。
    　　一产妇朝吐痰，夜发热，昼夜无寐，或用清痰降火，肌体日瘦，饮食日少，前证愈甚。余曰：早间吐痰，脾气虚也。夜间发热，肝血虚也。昼夜无寐，脾血耗也。遂用六君子汤、加味逍遥散、加味归脾汤，以次调补而痊。
    　　一产妇停食霍乱，用藿香正气散之类已愈，胸腹膨胀，饮食稍过，即呕吐或作泄泻，余谓此脾胃俱虚，用六君子汤加木香治之，渐愈。后因饮食失调，兼恚怒、患霍乱，胸腹大痛，手足逆冷，用附子散，又用八味丸，以补土母而康。设泥痛无补法，而用辛散或用平补之剂，必致不起。[299p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[299p]

    A woman of luxurious lifestyle experienced irregular menstruation after childbirth, with swollen and cracked lips and significant internal heat. She took cold remedies but later had intermittent discharges. I used a modified Qingwei Powder, and she recovered. Later, in spring, angered, her lips and mouth swelled, with cold and hot sensations causing vomiting, and minimal phlegm production. I prescribed Xiao Chaihu Decoction with added Gardenia, Poria, and Platycodon, and her symptoms resolved suddenly; however, internal heat persisted, so I adjusted her treatment with a modified Xiaoyao Powder to stabilize her.

    A postpartum woman coughed up phlegm in the morning, had fevers at night, and suffered from insomnia day and night, leading to significant weight loss and decreased appetite, with preceding symptoms worsening. I noted: morning phlegm signifies Spleen Qi deficiency. Night fevers indicate Liver Blood deficiency. Constant insomnia points to Spleen Blood consumption. I then used the Six Gentlemen Decoction, modified Xiaoyao Powder, and modified Gui Pi Decoction to restore her health progressively.

    A postpartum woman who had stopped eating due to cholera improved after using remedies like Huo Xiang Zheng Qi Powder. Yet she experienced abdominal bloating, with any excess food leading to vomiting or diarrhea. I suggested this was due to deficiency in both Spleen and Stomach, hence I prescribed the Six Gentlemen Decoction with added Mu Xiang, leading to gradual improvement. Later, due to dietary imbalance, combined with anger and cholera, she suffered from severe chest and abdominal pain, with cold limbs. I administered Fuzi Powder and Ba Wei Wan to replenish her Spleen. If treating pain without effective methods, using warming or gentle tonics could lead to complications.[299p]

    一产妇吐泻咽酸，面目浮肿，此脾气虚寒，先用六君子加炮姜为主，佐以越鞠丸而咽酸愈；又用补中益气加茯苓、半夏而脾胃康。
    　　一产妇患头痛，日用补中益气汤不缺，已三年矣，稍劳则恶寒内热，为阳气虚，以前汤加附子一钱，数剂不发。
    　　一妇人产后头痛面青二年矣，日服四物等药，余谓肾水不能生肝木而血虚，用六味丸加五味子，两月而痊。
    　　一产妇咳嗽声重，鼻塞流涕，此风寒所感，余用参苏饮一锺顿愈，与补中益气加桔梗、茯苓、半夏一剂而痊，又与六君子、黄芪以实腠理，痊愈。
    　　一产妇咳嗽痰盛，面赤口干，内热晡热，彻作无时，此阴火上炎，余用补中益气汤、六味地黄丸而愈。
    　　一产妇咳而腹满不食，涕唾面肿气逆、此病在胃而关于肺，用异功散而愈。
    　　一产妇泄泻，四肢面目浮肿，喘促恶寒，余谓脾肺虚寒，用六君子加姜桂而泄泻愈，又用补中益气而脾胃健。[300p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[300p]

    A woman in childbirth experienced vomiting and diarrhea, with a swollen face. This indicates a deficiency in the spleen and coldness. I used a prescription of Six Gentleman Decoction with roasted ginger as the main remedy, supplemented with Jue Ju Pill, which alleviated the acidic regurgitation; I then added Ren Shen to bolster the spleen and stomach.

    A woman in childbirth suffered from headaches and had been taking the Supplementing the Middle and Benefiting Qi Decoction daily without fail for three years. Whenever she exerted herself, she experienced internal chills and heat, indicating a deficiency in yang energy. I added a penny of Fu Zi to her previous decoction, and after a few doses, she did not experience any further issues.

    A woman suffered from postpartum headaches and a pale face for two years, taking Four Substance Decoction herbs daily. I believed that the kidney's water could not nourish the liver's wood, leading to blood deficiency. I prescribed the Six-Flavor Rehmannia Pill with Wu Wei Zi, and after two months, she recovered.

    A woman in childbirth had a heavy cough, nasal congestion, and a runny nose, indicating a wind-cold invasion. I used Shen Su Decoction for immediate relief and combined Supplementing the Middle and Benefiting Qi Decoction with Platycodon, Poria, and Ban Xia, leading to full recovery. I further combined Six Gentleman Decoction with Astragalus to strengthen the body's exterior, resulting in her complete healing.

    A woman in childbirth had a cough with abundant phlegm, a red face, dry mouth, and internal heat occurring at any time—this is due to ascending yin fire. I treated her with Supplementing the Middle and Benefiting Qi Decoction and Six-Flavor Rehmannia Pill, resulting in recovery.

    A woman in childbirth coughed while experiencing abdominal fullness and refused to eat, with swelling of the face and reverse flow of qi, indicating an illness in the stomach related to the lungs. I used Yi Gong San, and she recovered.

    A woman in childbirth had diarrhea, with swollen limbs and face, and difficulty breathing along with chills. I diagnosed this as a deficient cold in the spleen and lungs. I used Six Gentleman Decoction with added ginger and cinnamon, resulting in recovery from diarrhea, and subsequently used Supplementing the Middle and Benefiting Qi Decoction to strengthen the spleen and stomach.[300p]

    一产妇腹痛，发热恶食，气口脉大，余以为饮食伤脾，彼反服破血之剂，加寒热头痛，呕吐涎沫，用化痰降火理气，四肢逆冷，泄泻下坠。余曰：此脾胃虚之变证也，法当温补，用六君子加炮姜、肉桂、木香，再用补中益气汤而愈。
    　　一产妇腹痛，发热恶食，气口脉大，余以为饮食伤脾，彼反服破血之剂，加寒热头痛，呕吐涎沫，用化痰降火理气，四肢逆冷，泄泻下坠。余曰：此脾胃虚之变证也，法当温补，用六君子加炮姜、肉桂、木香，再用补中益气汤而愈。
    　　一产妇饮食少思，服消导之剂，四肢浮肿，余谓此乃中气不足所致，朝用补中益气汤，夕用六君子汤而愈。后因怒，致腹胀，误服沉香化气丸，吐泻不止，饮食不进，小便不利，肚腹四肢浮肿，用金匮加减肾气丸而愈。
    　　一妇产后痢，未至月满，因食冷物及酒，冷热与血攻击，滞下纯血，缠坠极痛，其脉大无力，口干，用黄芩芍药汤三服而安。[301p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[301p]

    A postpartum woman experienced abdominal pain, fever, aversion to food, and a strong pulse. I believed this was due to dietary damage to the spleen, but she instead took a blood-breaking remedy, which worsened her cold and hot headaches and caused vomiting of foamy saliva. She also had cold extremities, diarrhea, and a feeling of heaviness. I said: This is a transformation of spleen and stomach deficiency. The treatment should be warming and tonifying, using the Six Gentlemen Decoction with roasted ginger, cinnamon, and patchouli, followed by the Recovery of Vitality Decoction to achieve recovery.

    Another postpartum woman had little appetite and was anxious, took digestive remedies, and had swelling in her limbs. I determined this was caused by insufficient central qi, so I administered the Recovery of Vitality Decoction in the morning and the Six Gentlemen Decoction in the evening, leading to her recovery. Later, due to anger, she developed abdominal distension and mistakenly took a pill to transform qì which caused continuous vomiting and diarrhea, inability to ingest food, difficulty urinating, and swelling in her abdomen and limbs. I treated her with modifications of the Kidney Qi Pill, leading to recovery.

    A woman had dysentery after childbirth, not yet a full month postpartum, due to consuming cold foods and alcohol, causing a clash of cold and heat with blood stagnation, leading to pure blood in her stools, extreme pain, a strong but weak pulse, and dryness of the mouth. I administered Huangqin and Shaoyao Decoction three times, and she stabilized.[301p]

    一产妇泄痢腹痛年余，形体骨立，内热晡热，自汗盗汗，口舌糜烂，口吐痰三碗许，脉洪大，重按全无，此命门火衰而假热，脾土虚寒，不能摄痰归源，用八味丸补火以生土，用补中益气汤兼补肺金而痊。
    　　一产妇食鸡子，腹中作痛，面色青黄，服平胃、二陈，更下痢腹胀，用流气饮子；又小腹一块，不时上攻，饮食愈少，此脾胃虚寒，肝木克侮所致，用补中益气加木香、吴茱萸渐愈；又用八珍大补，兼服调理，寻愈。
    　　一产妇泻痢发热，作渴吐痰，肌体消瘦，饮食少思，或胸膈痞满，或小腹胀坠，年余矣，余以为脾肾之泻，朝用二神丸，夕用六君子，三月余而痊。
    　　一产妇腹痛后重，去痢无度，形体倦怠，饮食不进，兴死为邻，此脾胃俱虚也。用四神丸十全大补汤而愈；但饮食难化，肢体倦怠，再补中益气汤调理而康。[302p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[302p]

    A woman in childbirth experienced diarrhea and abdominal pain for over a year, with her body appearing emaciated, internal heat and fever in the afternoons, spontaneous sweating and night sweats, a raw mouth and tongue, and expectorating about three bowls of phlegm. Her pulse was strong but when pressed hard, it was absent, indicating a decline in the fire of the life gate and false heat, while the spleen was weak and cold, unable to control phlegm. The Eight Flavor Pill was used to strengthen the fire and generate earth, along with the Middle-Ground Qi-Enhancing Decoction to also nourish the lung metal, leading to recovery.

    Another woman who ate eggs experienced abdominal pain, a pale yellow complexion, and after taking the Pingwei Decoction and Erchen Decoction, she developed diarrhea and abdominal distension. A flow gas drink was used; however, the lower abdomen had a persistent discomfort that occasionally surged, with dwindling appetite. This was caused by weak and cold spleen and stomach along with liver wood oppression. The Middle-Ground Qi-Enhancing Decoction was supplemented with cardamom and wuzhuyu, leading to gradual improvement; eight treasures were also used for a significant boost, along with adjustments, resulting in recovery.

    A woman with diarrhea, fever, and thirst sought relief from phlegm, showing significant weight loss, a diminished desire for food, occasional chest distention, or a sense of heaviness in the lower abdomen, persisting for over a year. I diagnosed it as a spleen-kidney issue, administering the Ershen Pill in the morning and the Liu Junzi Decoction in the evening, leading to recovery in a little over three months.

    Another woman had severe abdominal pain with excessive diarrhea, enduring fatigue and refusal of food, nearly succumbing to distress. This indicated deficiency in both spleen and stomach. I prescribed the Four Shen Pill and the Total Reinforcement Decoction for healing; however, with persistent dietary digestion issues and fatigue in her limbs, further treatment with the Middle-Ground Qi-Enhancing Decoction was necessary for her recovery.[302p]

    一产妇患疟，久不愈，百病蜂起，其脉或洪大，或微细，或弦紧，或沉伏，难以名状，用六君子加炮姜二十余剂，脉证稍得；又用参朮煎膏佐以归脾汤，百余剂而瘥。
    　　陈氏妇张，素怯弱，生女自乳，因病疥年余，遂致羸困，后因执丧礼劳顿，数欲眩仆。一日感气，忽患心脾高肿作疼，手不可按，呕吐不止，六脉微细之极。陈翁自以脉虽虚而病形则实，误以诸痛不可补气，乃用青皮、香附、吴萸等药而愈。继复患疟，且堕胎，又自投理气行血之药，病去元气转脱，再投参、芪补剂不应矣。六脉如丝欲绝，思非附子不能起，因亟请余诊之。余知此由理气损真之误也，连投参、芪、归、朮、附子、姜、桂六剂，间用八味丸，五日眠食渐甘，六脉全复。余论之云：向使心脾疼时，即服此药，疟亦不作矣。[303p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[303p]

    A woman in childbirth suffered from malaria, which persisted for a long time, leading to the onset of numerous other ailments. Her pulse varied between strong and large, thin and weak, tight and wiry, or sinking and hidden, making it hard to diagnose. After administering more than twenty doses of the Six Gentlemen Decoction with roasted ginger, her pulse and symptoms improved slightly. Subsequently, she was given ginseng and atractylodes decoction combined with Gui Pi Decoction. After over a hundred doses, she recovered.

    Mrs. Zhang from the Chen family was naturally timid and weak. After giving birth and nursing a daughter, she developed a skin ailment that lasted over a year, which led to her becoming emaciated. Later, after enduring the fatigue of funeral rites, she frequently felt faint and about to collapse. One day she was affected by an epidemic, suddenly experiencing severe swelling and pain in her heart and spleen, with her hands being too tender to touch and persistent vomiting. Her six pulses were extremely weak and thin. Mr. Chen mistakenly believed that despite her weak pulse, the nature of her illness was substantial and incorrectly thought that the pain shouldn't be treated by tonifying qi. He then used herbs like Qingpi, Xiangfu, and Wuyue, which resulted in recovery.

    However, she subsequently suffered from malaria and had a miscarriage. She then self-prescribed medications to regulate qi and move blood, which depleted her vital energy further, and the tonics of ginseng and astragalus proved ineffective. Her six pulses became extremely thin and seemed on the verge of vanishing. She felt that only aconite could revive her, so she urgently sought my diagnosis. I recognized that her condition was the result of a mistake with qi regulation damaging her essence. I promptly prescribed a combination of ginseng, astragalus, angelica, atractylodes, aconite, ginger, and cassia in six doses, interspersed with the Eight Treasure Pill. After five days, her sleep and appetite gradually improved, and her six pulses completely recovered. I commented that had she taken this medicine when she first felt the heart and spleen pain, the malaria would not have occurred.[303p]

    一产妇大便七日不通，饮贪如常，腹中如故，此腹未满也。用八珍加桃杏二仁，至二十一日腹满欲去，用猪胆汁润去而安。
    　　一产妇大便八日不通，或用通利之药，中脘胀痛，饮食甚少；又用蜜导之，大便不禁，呃逆不食。余以为脾肾复伤，用六君子加茱萸、肉果、补骨脂、五味数剂，喜其年壮而愈。不然，多致不起。
    　　一产妇大便秘结，小腹胀痛，用大黄等药，致吐泻不食，腹痛胸痞，余用六君子加木香、炮姜治之而愈。
    　　一产妇大小便不通，诸药不应，将危矣。令饮牛乳，一日稍通，三日而痊。人乳尤善。
    　　一产妇大便不实，饮食少思，五更或侵晨遗屎，此中气虚寒、脾肾不足，用补中益气送四神丸而痊。[304p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[304p]

    A woman in childbirth has been unable to defecate for seven days, eats and drinks as usual, and her abdomen feels the same; this indicates her abdomen is not full. Use the Eight Treasures with the addition of two peach and apricot kernels. On the twenty-first day, her abdomen feels full and she wishes to expel it; use pig bile to lubricate and relieve her.

    A woman in childbirth has not defecated for eight days. If purgative medicine is used, there may be distension and pain in the middle abdomen, with very little food intake; even with honey as a guide, the constipation persists along with hiccups and lack of appetite. I believe this has further injured the spleen and kidneys, so I use Six Gentlemen's Decoction with additions of evodia, myrtle fruit, tonifying psoralea, and several other ingredients, and she recovers because she is still young. Otherwise, it could lead to serious issues.

    A woman in childbirth has constipation and experiences swelling and pain in the lower abdomen. Using rhubarb and other medications may cause vomiting and diarrhea with loss of appetite, along with abdominal pain and chest oppression. I treated her with Six Gentlemen's Decoction plus saussurea and prepared ginger, and she recovered.

    A woman in childbirth has both large and small intestions unable to function, and various medicines are ineffective, leading to danger. I instructed her to drink cow's milk, and within one day there was some relief, and she fully recovered within three days. Human milk is even better.

    A woman in childbirth has unsatisfactory bowel movements, has a decreased appetite, and may accidentally pass stool around dawn or early morning. This indicates deficiency and coldness in the middle qi, and insufficiency of the spleen and kidneys. I used a traditional remedy that nourishes the middle and benefits qi, along with the medicinal formula of the Four Spirits, and she recovered.[304p]

    一产妇小便出粪，名大小肠交，乃气血俱虚，失行常道，先用六君子汤二剂，又用五苓散二剂而痊。寻常肠交方可用。
    　　一产妇小水淋沥，或时自出，用分利降火之剂，二年不愈。余以为肺肾之气虚，用补中益气汤、六味地黄丸而痊。
    　　一产妇小便频数，时忽寒战，乃属脾肺虚弱，用补中益气加山茱、山药为主，佐以桑螵蛸散而愈。后患发热晡热，盗汗自汗，月水不调，用加味逍遥散而安。
    　　一产妇患前证，吐痰发热，日晡作渴，此膀胱阴虚，用补中益气汤，佐以六味丸而愈。又患痢后小便频数，手足俱冷，属阳气虚寒，用前汤及八味丸而瘳。
    　　一产妇小便不禁，三年不愈，面色或青赤，或黄白，此肝脾气血虚热，用加味逍遥散为主渐愈，佐以六味丸而痊。后因怒，小便自遗，大便不实，左目顿紧，面色顿赤，仍用前散，佐以六君子汤，以清肝火，生肝血，培脾土而瘳。[305p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[305p]

    A woman in childbirth experienced urination mixed with feces, known as the intersection of the large and small intestines, caused by deficiency of both qi and blood, and the loss of normal functions. She was first treated with two doses of the Six Gentlemen Decoction, followed by two doses of the Five Ling Powder, resulting in recovery. Typically, the intersection of the intestines can be treated with such methods.

    Another woman in childbirth suffered from intermittent urinary dribbling, which occurred irregularly. A diuretic to relieve heat was used, but after two years, there was no improvement. I believed it was due to deficiency of the lung and kidney qi, and treated her with the Revive the Middle and Boost Qi Decoction and the Six-Flavor Rehmannia Pill, resulting in recovery.

    One woman in childbirth had frequent urination, sometimes accompanied by chills and shivering, attributed to weakness of the spleen and lungs. I used the Revive the Middle and Boost Qi Decoction, adding shan zhu and shan yao as primary herbs, and supplemented with the mulberry cicada shell powder, leading to her recovery. Later, she experienced fever in the afternoon, night sweats, and irregular menstruation, for which I prescribed an enhanced version of the Free and Easy Wanderer Powder, resulting in stability.

    Another woman presented similar symptoms as before, with phlegm and fever, thirst during the day; this indicated a deficiency of the bladder yin. I administered the Revive the Middle and Boost Qi Decoction, supplemented with the Six-Flavor Pill, and she recovered. However, she later suffered from frequent urination after dysentery, with cold hands and feet, indicating deficiency and cold of yang qi. I treated her with the previous decoction and the Eight Flavor Pill, leading to recovery.

    One woman experienced uncontrollable urination for three years, with a complexion that varied from bluish-red to yellowish-white. This was due to a deficiency of the liver and spleen qi, blood, and heat. I used the enhanced version of the Free and Easy Wanderer Powder as the main treatment, gradually leading to recovery, and supplemented with the Six-Flavor Pill. Later, due to anger, she had involuntary urination and loose stools, with sudden tightness in the left eye and a flushed complexion. I treated her again with the previous powder, along with the Six Gentlemen Decoction, to clear liver fire, nourish liver blood, and bolster spleen function, resulting in her recovery.[305p]

    一产妇尿血面黄，胁胀少食，此肝乘脾也，用加味逍遥、补中益气兼服而愈。后为怀抱不乐，食少体倦，惊悸无寐，血仍作，用加味归脾汤二十余剂，将愈，惑于众论，服犀角地黄汤，诸证复作，仍服前汤而愈。
    　　一产妇粪后下血，食少体倦，此脾气虚热，用补中益气加吴茱萸、炒黄连五分，四剂顿愈；但怔忡少寐，盗汗未止，用归脾汤而痊。
    　　一妇人久下血，在粪前，属脾肾虚寒，元气下陷，用补中益气加黄连、炒吴茱萸一钱，数剂稍缓；乃加生吴茱萸五分数剂而愈。
    　　一产妇大便后血，口干饮汤，胸胁膨满，小腹闷坠，内热晡热，食少体倦，面色痿黄，日晡则赤，洒淅恶寒，此脾肺气虚，先用六君子加干姜、木香渐愈，用补中益气将愈，用归脾汤痊愈。后饮食失节，劳役兼怒，发热血崩，夜间讝语，此热入血室，用加味小柴胡二剂而热退，用补中益气一剂而血止，用逍遥散、归脾汤调理而安。[306p]

    Reasoning Trace

    This is a placeholder for a reasoning trace of the translation, describing in detail how the translation was created.[306p]
